title: Ribbed Fingerless Gloves
description: "These crochet ribbed fingerless gloves are made with medium-weight yarn and a 4.5 mm hook. The ribbed texture creates a stretchy, comfortable fit that keeps hands warm while leaving fingers free. The gloves are worked flat and then seamed, making the construction simple and beginner-friendly. The seam is nearly invisible, giving the gloves a clean and polished look. This practical and stylish accessory is perfect for everyday wear during chilly autumn and winter days. Happy Crocheting!"

title: Glamorous Pearl Center Flowers
description: This pattern is so short and sweet, you will definitely do a double-take. The Glamorous Pearl Center Flowers are inspired by hydrangeas and the rich purple colors of their interlocking petals that can form lovely round bouquets. These simple crochet flowers get their extra pop from adding a pearl center to the four-petal design. These centers could be adjusted to a smaller, purple bead to look more like hydrangeas, or you can keep the large white pearl to really make a glamorous statement. Whatever route you choose, the crochet flower pattern will be a delightfully easy project for anyone craving their floral fix.

title: Sargassum
description: "Capture the feeling of delicate, lacy strands of seaweed, white sand beaches littered with shells, and the sparkle of sunshine on the clear, blue ocean. Sargassum is the name of a seaweed that washes ashore on the gulf beaches of Florida, where I recently went for a vacation. The days were full of warm sunshine, but there was still enough of a chill in the early mornings and late evenings to make this shawl a welcome accessory. The striping pattern gently shifts from more green to more blue, without repeating, while optional beads add a nice weight, and occasional sparkle as they catch the sunlight."

title: The Shell Fold Napkin Edging | Free Crochet Pattern
description:
"There’s something quietly beautiful about the small details that bring a table together—the kind that feel effortless, yet completely intentional.

This crochet-edged linen napkin was inspired by a moment in Chapter 2 of The Sea Glass Journal, where a simple folding technique transforms an ordinary cloth into something sculptural and refined. With just a few folds and a delicate shell edging, the napkin settles into shape naturally—soft, balanced, and unexpectedly elegant.

The edging is worked in a classic shell pattern using cotton yarn, adding texture and a subtle decorative finish without overwhelming the simplicity of the linen. Paired with an accordion-style fold and a napkin ring, it becomes a beautiful detail for everyday dining or special occasions alike.

Whether you’re setting a table for guests or simply elevating a quiet meal at home, this design is a reminder that the smallest creative touches can make a lasting impression.

The Story Behind the Design

In Chapter 2 of The Sea Glass Journal, the task seems simple—prepare the table. But like so many moments in the story, it’s the quiet transformation of something ordinary that stands out.

“She wanted cloth—folded. Something sculptural…”

With a few deliberate movements, the napkin becomes more than functional. It becomes part of the atmosphere—something thoughtful, something beautiful, something just right.

This pattern was created to capture that feeling.

If you’d like to experience the full moment that inspired this design, you can watch Chapter 2 here:"

title: Frost Blossom Cowl Crochet Pattern
description:
"Wrap yourself in cozy texture with the Frost Blossom Cowl, a beautifully simple crochet pattern designed for comfort, style, and everyday wear.
This cowl is part of the Frost Blossom Collection, a special collaboration between Vicky and her daughter Casey. Using the same stitch and yarn combination as the rest of the collection, this piece brings that signature “frost meets bloom” look into a wearable accessory you’ll reach for all season long.
The pattern features the lemon peel stitch—a relaxing combination of single and double crochet stitches that creates a soft, textured fabric with a woven look. It’s easy to learn, enjoyable to repeat, and perfect for crocheters of all skill levels.
This cowl is worked flat in rows and then seamed, making construction simple while still giving you a polished finished piece.
"

title: Lilienne Poncho
description:
"The Lilienne Poncho is a lightweight rectangle poncho that’s simple to crochet and easy to wear.

Worked in one piece with an airy mesh yoke and textured crossed stitches, it creates a beautiful drape while staying light and breathable.

The design can be worn two ways, giving you a choice of neckline styles with the same piece.

One size fits most body types. Front and back panels measure 38 inches each (76 inches total circumference).

An easy, go-to piece that feels just as lovely to wear as it is to crochet.

Skill level : Beginner Friendly

title: Small Alligator
description:
"Introducing the Small Crochet Alligator! This cutie is quick to make and full of personality, featuring the option to make him all one color or add a colored tummy in the color of your choosing. Its compact size makes it ideal for using up leftover yarn while creating something irresistibly cute.

Whether you keep it as a miniature plush or attach it to a keychain to carry a bit of handmade happiness wherever you go, this little alligator is sure to bring smiles. With simple shaping and playful details, it’s a fun project that works up quickly and makes a delightful addition to any crochet collection, a gift for someone, or to be sold at craft fairs. 
PATTERN INFORMATION
+ The pattern is written in English using US crochet terminology.

SKILLS NEEDED
+ The skill level needed is advanced beginner.  
+ The techniques you will need to know include chain, single crochet, slip stitch, single crochet increase and decrease, invisible decrease, working in the back bar of the ch, bobble stitch, starting an oval piece with a foundation chain, and the adjustable (magic) ring. 

MATERIALS NEEDED (more specifics are noted within the pattern)
+ yarn in your choice of colors (about 44 yds/40 m of worsted/aran)
+ a size E/4 (3.5 mm) crochet hook
+ stitch markers 
+ tapestry needle 
+ stuffing
+ pins
+ scissors 
+ safety eyes
+ chopstick to help stuff

FINISHED MEASUREMENTS
+ Using hook size E/4 (3.5 mm) and worsted yarn, the Small Crochet Alligator measures approx. 3.25 inches (8.25 cm) tall.

title: How To Overlay Mosaic Crochet Without Cutting Yarn
description:
"In this tutorial you’ll learn how to overlay mosaic crochet without cutting yarn.

If you love overlay mosaic crochet, but don’t like all the ends to weave in, then you’ve come to the right place.

While mosaic crochet can be fun & amazing, there’s no secret that the amount of yarn ends to weave in is a bit of a downside. There’s some great techniques that solves that issue, like creating fringe with the ends to weave in or a double layered crocheted border.

But isn’t it better to avoid cutting the yarn at all? Let’s dive into this technique."

title: Crochet Phone Pocket
description:
"Create a stylish and practical crochet phone pocket with this easy and beginner-friendly pattern. This crochet cellphone pouch is designed to keep your phone safe while adding a touch of handmade charm to your everyday accessories. With simple stitches and a compact design, it’s a fun project for anyone who loves making useful crochet items. Whether you’re heading out for a quick errand, a walk, or a casual day out, this crochet phone purse keeps your essentials close and organized.

If you enjoy making crochet phone accessories, this project is both practical and creative. It’s also a wonderful handmade gift idea for friends, teens, and family members who appreciate useful DIY items. Small crochet projects like this are ideal for using leftover yarn while creating something beautiful and functional.

Whether you call it a crochet cellphone pouch, crochet earphone pouch, crochet wallet purse, or crochet phone bag, this pattern is a simple and satisfying make. Add this cute DIY phone pouch to your list of crochet projects and enjoy crafting an accessory you can use every day."

title: How To Crochet The Woodland Stitch
description:
"Keep reading to learn how to crochet the woodland stitch. This crochet stitch pattern might be known under other names. However like many of my other favorite stitches, I don’t remember where I first came across it. The Woodland stitch gives a lovely texture & quite a “romantic” & traditional crochet look. It is a fan stitch & it is quite easy.

If you have bought one of my crochet patterns, you might wonder how it is done. The pattern always offers written text in addition to charts, but now I will give you a lot of additional explanations. In addition there is a video of how to crochet this stitch on the bottom.

Today I suggest that you learn the stitch & then you should use it in a project! You can make you own project, but on the bottom of this post I have also included all crochet patterns that are using the woodland stitch."

title: Tulip Coaster Crochet Pattern
description:
"Bring a touch of spring to your home with this Tulip Coaster Crochet Pattern! These cheerful crochet tulip coasters are made with simple stitches and are quick and enjoyable to make. Once you get the rhythm of the pattern, you can finish a coaster in about 30 minutes, making this a wonderfully satisfying project when you’re in the mood for something quick and creative. The pattern includes clear written instructions along with a step-by-step video tutorial to guide you through the entire process.

Tulips are one of my favorite flowers, and designing these coasters was such a joy. I’ve always loved how tulips signal the arrival of spring after a long winter, and I wanted to create a crochet design that captures that same bright and uplifting feeling.

These coasters are also perfect for using up small bits of scrap yarn from other projects. Since they work up so quickly, it’s easy to crochet several in one sitting and make a whole set. They’re wonderful for gifting, adding a handmade touch to your home, or even making multiples to sell at craft shows."

title: Top Deck Tee
description:
"If you’re looking for a fun and easy crochet project for summer, this top-down raglan crochet top is a great one to try. This easy crochet summer top free pattern is beginner friendly and worked in one piece from the neckline down, so there’s no seaming needed at all. The raglan construction creates a comfortable fit across the shoulders, and the simple stitches make it a really relaxing project. The striped crochet top pattern adds a playful pop of colour, but you can easily customise it with your own favourite shades.
This summer top crochet pattern is perfect if you’re making your first crochet garment. Since it’s worked from the top down, you can try it on as you go and adjust the length to suit you. The crochet tee pattern includes sizes XS, S, M, L, XL, 2XL, 3XL, 4XL and 5XL, so you can find the fit that works best. If you’ve been wanting to try making a wearable piece, this beginner friendly crochet pattern is a great place to start."

title: Ancel Wrap
description: Looking for a fun fall accessory? This cozy wrap features a joyful plaid pattern the perfect thing to wear on a hayride, or to a football game in your team's colors. The pretty plaid is crocheted by using two colors of yarn held double, then alternating a single strand to make the color change. Worked flat, in the intarsia method, the pattern is a repetition of 9 sts and 7 rows, with yarn held double. MC is comprised of one strand of C1 and one strand of C2, while C1 holds two strands of C1 together and C2 holds two strands of C2 together. C1 should be the beginning and ending color of each row as this will create the solid color edging.

title: Bobble Soap Sack
description: Designed to support the mission of nonprofit organization S.A.C.K. (Supporting A Community with Kindness). This organization's vision is to inspire donations of bar soap to local food pantries, homeless shelters, social service agencies, and veterans clinics. By crocheting a soap sack and pairing it with a sealed bar of soap, you can give essential toiletries and a lovingly handmade item to those who need them. Find out more at soapsacks.org. This pattern is fun to crochet, and results in a soap sack with a scrubby side, thanks to the bobble stitches on the front. The bobbles provide a focal point, and a catalyst for creativity as you mix and match yarns to create unique soap sacks. You'll want to make these for gifts as well as donations to deserving organizations. The soap sack is crocheted as a large rectangle and then folded and seamed using a sl st. A long chain creates a drawstring that is threaded after assembly.

title: Bold and Bright Scarf
description: Fight the cold in color with a chunky, chill–chasing crocheted scarf! title: Bun Bun Bunny
description: Bun-Bun Bunny is the perfect childhood companion. A soft and furry plush who is entirely made in crochet, hidden underneath the fur. Add a jacket to enhance the handmade toy feel. This project uses the “amigurumi technique” of holding together a plain and a fur yarn. It only uses single crochet stitches worked in rounds. The head and body are worked in one piece starting at the top of the head. Then ears, arms, legs, and tail are sewn to the body. A portion of the ears and the muzzle fur is shortened using sharp scissors.

title: Calathea Cardigan
description: New leaves are one of the most promising and inspiring symbols of spring. Calathea Cardigan highlights a beautiful combination of stitches which create a wide, solid-leaf border and a large, lace-leaf body. The leaves are formed by special composite stitches (groups of stitches linked together mid-way) which suspend the leaves in a circular web of chain spaces. The stitch guide provides explanation for the composite stitches used in the pattern and a chart helps to visualize them. The duster also showcases a striking effect of thread crochet lace in a comfortable weight. Open front and high slits at the sides of the duster create a flowing silhouette accommodating for many body types.

title: Color Me Quick Vest
description: Basic stitches in super bulky combine to create a fast and fun crocheted vest! The simple straight–edged silhouette doesn’t require increasing or decreasing. That means fuss-free stitching for both new and experienced crocheters. Vest is worked back–and–forth in rows in one piece from bottom to armholes. Then the piece is divided and the fronts worked separately from the back. After stitching is complete, the shoulders are sewn together to join. Sizes included: 27.75 (30.5, 33.75, 39, 42.5)(45, 50.25, 53.5, 57)” finished chest; meant to fit 28–30 (32–34, 36–38, 40–42, 44–46)(48–50, 52–54, 56–58, 60–62)” chest circumference. Shown in size 33.75."

title: Coloratura Lace Top
description: The Coloratura Lace Top features a delicate shell stitch in a range of colors that is set against a solid accent color. This top makes a sophisticated addition to your summer wardrobe. The top is worked from the bottom up, initially in the round and then in rows for the front and back of the chest, which are identical rectangles. Finally, the front and back are joined at each shoulder, creating a boat neck. The top features a four-row lace repeat alternating with two rows of solid double crochet. The pattern uses one main color and several different accent colors. Use a different accent color for each repeat of Rounds 3-6 in the main body and Rows 1-4 (or Rows 7, 2-4) of the front and back panels. Use the same color sequence for the front and back panels. Some colors used in the sample are no longer available. We have made substitution suggestions in the Kit Builder.

description: This is a great bag to hold all the essentials for a walk around town, the beach or the local craft and antiques market. The Darcy Tote bag is a quick and easy pattern to make. It is worked in two identical panels that are then joined on three sides by single crochet. Snap on a purchased strap and it is ready to go!

title: Dazzler the Unicorn Headband
description: Who doesn't want to be a unicorn? This playful headband fits children as well as adults because why should the kids shouldn't have all the fun. Great for birthday celebrations, dance parties, or any day when you need a little extra magic. The way this horn spirals as it grows from your hands feels just like magic. The spiraling happens as a result of a few increases right next to a few decreases. Be careful not to miss the first half of the first decrease, since it does get a little crowded with all those increase stitches next to it. A crocheted circle gets folded in half around a half-circle of plastic canvas and is stitched around the center of a plastic headband to create the base. This allows more room to attach the horn and flowers and helps keep the headband upright.

title: Eventide Skirt
description: There's nothing like a cool breeze in the hot summer. This lovely crochet skirt was inspired by the look of the evening wind blowing through waves of grass on the seashore. The skirt is worked from the top town. It is worked flat for the belt band and first 4 rows of the skirt. The rest of the skirt is joined in rounds and worked back and forth. Increases are worked evenly in each pattern repeat. Follow the numbers in the brackets when increasing.

title: Facets Cowl
description: Dovetailed stripes show off the jewel-like colors of Chroma as they emerge, reminiscent of the vibrant, unpredictable gradations of agate or tourmaline. Worked in a spiral with nothing more complex than single crochet and chain spaces, Facets offers eye-catching results even to the novice crocheter; a small repeat and a carnival of color choices make it ultimately customizable. Try a solid and a variegated yarn together for sparkling contrast, coordinating solids for classic stripes, or two self-striping colorways to make a dazzling statement. Quick to make and quicker to wear, Facets is sure to become your go-to project for accessorizing your wardrobe, chasing the chills, or giving away. Note: Some of the original colors are no longer available and we have made a suggestion of comparable colors in the Choose Colors section, but it will not be an exact match to the sample shown in the image.

description: Greet cooler weather with a quick and cozy crocheted cowl! Ideally suited for heavy worsted or light bulky weight yarn, this piece is a versatile way to accessorize an outfit. description: Brighten the kitchen with a fanciful, flowered dishcloth or trivet! title: Forest of Dean Shawl
description: The ancient woodland of the Forest of Dean in Gloucestershire, England was the inspiration for this shawl. Rows of DC stitches represent a deep forest of trees, lacy shells and V stitches evoke leafy treetops and shrubberies, and the chain space line suggests a river running through the forest. The Forest of Dean Shawl is worked as a side-to-side triangle. Starting at one side with a single V stitch, each subsequent row increases at the neckline to expand into a lower section of Lace and an upper section of DC. The final long row at the opposite end is decorated with crossed DC and lacy shells.

title: Forte Filet Tee
description: The Forte Filet Tee features a triangular filet lace pattern in the back which gives the top a modern look. Crochet it in cotton thread to create a comfortable garment for hot summer days. This crochet top is shaped in a flowing A-line silhouette and a slightly longer back. Front and back are worked seamlessly from hem to armhole in turned rounds with the beginning of round at front center, with a puff stitch hiding round joins. This design is filled with attention to detail: the filet design initially overflows to the front and gradually recedes to the sides, the back features a small box pleat at the top, and small cap sleeves finish the design.

title: Funicular Cowl
description: Interlocking columns of left- and right-twisting cables evoke the structure and movement of a cable-operated mountain railroad, which is where this cowl gets its fun name. The post-stitch cables reach upward from the bottom edge of the cowl, giving it rich allover texture. The Funicular Cowl is a tubular design worked in rounds from bottom to top. The cable pattern is created by crossing groups of three Treble Crochet post stitches over one another. A round of Single Crochet gives the top edge a clean finish.

title: Hue Shift Crochet Afghan
description: Eleven colors blend harmoniously in this mitered afghan! This afghan is crocheted in four pieces and seamed together. Each of the four pieces begins at one corner, and mitered squares are crocheted directly onto the existing work. Made with simple single crochet mitered squares and finished off with a darker border, the Hue Shift Afghan is a deceptively simple pattern that explores color in a new and creative way! Note: Working at gauge, a single color square will use 10 grams of yarn. Each color is used for 1 single color square and 18 additional squares where it is paired with another color. This means each of the 10 main body colors will use all 100 grams of the skein. We have suggested an additional skein for each color, to make sure the entire pattern can be completed.

title: Nightfall Sweater
description: Inspired by the curve of a classical vase with ornate decorations, the Nightfall Sweater features an array of light cables against a chain mesh background. Loose fit and high hip length make this sweater an easy layering companion for a variety of outfits. The cables create texture and do not weigh down the fabric, and the fabric grows quickly with tall stitches and sport weight yarn. The sweater is worked in two pieces from the bottom up. Ribbing is worked with a smaller hook for slight shaping. The front and back are split in half in the middle for the neck and worked straight up. When the halves part, they create a v-neck opening, shoulder slopes, and a slight hi-lo effect at the hem. Shoulders are seamed. Drop-shoulder sleeves are worked separately and seamed to the body. The height of the post stitches is varied to achieve an airy elongated look and balance gauge across different sections. Sizes are graded by adding extra stitches to side borders and/or additional narrow cables.

One of the yarns used in the sample is no longer available; we have made some substitution suggestions in the Kit Builder.

title: Rose Window Top
description: A single flower outline against a shimmering silk background creates a clean and organic look. Minimalistic edging and a modern slightly cropped silhouette with plenty of ease complete the image of soft and natural elegance. title: Rosy Romper
description: This comfy outfit is classic and laid back, perfect for taking baby for a walk on the beach or a stroll on the boardwalk. This romper has a functional and adorable button-front closure for diaper changing access. The Rosy Romper is worked in one piece, starting with the front bib, decreasing and increasing for the crotch area, working the arm shaping and straps, then lastly, the back center. The Rosy Romper is worked in a fingering weight yarn for a lightweight fabric and comfortable fit. Each edge of the front panel uses slip stitches for a horizontal rib look that also helps shape the romper around the baby’s bottom.

title: Sea Star Cardi
description: This light and easy-to-wear, feminine and flattering cardigan will become your favorite beach cover-up or maybe just your favorite summer garment! The motifs easily blend together to form a simple and beautiful flower-patterned fabric. Construction begins with the filet mesh waistband; then, the lower part is worked from top to bottom. Motifs are made and joined in horizontal rows, which are then joined to the main part. The top part is constructed similarly in rows, from the waist up, separating front and back at the underarm and joining motifs at the shoulders.

title: Sparkles the Unicorn Softie
description: Have you hugged your unicorn today? This extraordinary creature is said to have magical healing powers within its horn. And who doesn't need a bit of magic from time to time? The rainbow hooves and mane are extra cheerful, perking up any room where this unicorn resides. The spiraling horn happens as a result of a few increases right next to a few decreases. Be careful not to miss the first half of the first decrease as it's easy to miss. Within the directions for the hooves are instruction for a join that reduces the appearance of a jog in color. A little crocheted strip, made in rainbow colors takes the guesswork out of adding the mane to the head. Simply attach the fringe to the mane following the colors in the strip, then sew the strip to the head.

title: Spindrift Cardigan
description: Inspired by a sunset over the ocean, this pretty cardigan starts out with a neutral-colored body that is energized by stripes of color near the hem. Lace rows dance like the spray off an ocean wave. The cardigan is worked from the bottom up. The lower body is worked back and forth in one piece to underarm. Then the front and back panels are worked separately. Sleeves are worked back and forth in pieces, then seamed together and set into armhole. Make 5 buttonholes along the right front edge when working the edge.

title: Spiral Tunisian Cowl
description: The Spiral Tunisian Cowl is a sophisticated and modern cowl that uses simple Tunisian crochet stitches, which makes it a great introduction pattern if you want to learn Tunisian crochet. The cowl is worked in the round. The 10 stitch and 10 rows repeating YO eyelet pattern is worked from top to bottom. To adjust size for a larger cowl, increase the starting chain by a multiple of 10 stitches. Each 10-stitch repeat will add approximately 3.5" to the circumference.

title: Stitch Your State: Warm Up America!by Knit Picks Design Team
description: The Great Yarn Challenge (TGYC) is hosted by the Craft Yarn Council and is a series of challenges based around different themes. Knit Picks and WeCrochet partnered with CYC to bring you the Stitch Your State: Warm Up America! challenge. We have designed 51 charts (1 for each state plus DC) to be used in a variety of ways. Each measures 9"x7" so they are perfect for Warm Up America! blocks. You can find more information about The Great Yarn Challenge HERE. There are basic instructions for both knit and crochet to get you started and to finish, then you pick your state chart to work from. For these charts, the white boxes are the background, and the dark boxes are the shape of the state. We've also included a yellow box for each state capital! Once you have finished your state, now the fun begins! Choose some images to show what your state means to you - you can use simple images such as clip art or go as detailed as you want! Use state flowers, state birds, tourist attractions and anything that makes your state home. Having trouble coming up with some images? Here's what we did: For our home state of Washington, we wanted to represent our office in Vancouver and naturally, the Space Needle in Seattle. And, of course, we had to include some trees for the Evergreen state! Ohio is as familiar to us as Washington is, since our warehouse is there! It's represented with Cedar Point Amusement Park. We've also included the Rock n' Roll Hall of fame in Cleveland (rocks!) and the state flower, the Red Carnation. Designer Alison Griffith used to spend vacations in South Carolina watching the dolphins, so she had to include that to represent SC. The state flag features a palmetto tree and a white crescent so those are stitched here too!

title: Summer Days Top
description: Inspired by vintage fashions and intricate patterns of repeating lace, the Summer Days Top is a visual lace experience. This repeating pattern cascades in a display of delicate romance. The body of the garment is a lace pattern consisting of a multiple of six stitches plus one. This pattern has a two-row repeat. As the number of stitches between the two rows is different, the pattern follows the stitch count on the even rows. For shaping the shoulders and sleeves, decreases are worked within the two-row repeat, decreasing six stitches per row.

title: Washington Park Cowl-Neck Sweater
description: There's always something to do at the park! Whether you're watching kids at the playground or walking the trails, you need something cute to wear on your outing. The Washington Park Cowl Neck Sweater is fun to make and even more fun to wear: a variety of stitches will keep you entertained throughout the project. Front, back, and sleeves are worked separately from bottom up. Sleeves are easily seamed to body in a drop shoulder construction. Relaxed fit supports a variety of sizes and preferred ease. The sweater grows fast with medium weight yarn and a 5mm hook, and the fabric is light and pliable. Wide cowl neck seamlessly continues from body to collar.

description: A beautiful ode to all things fall, the Wheat Vest reminds us of the soft rustling of the wheat fields and harvest time with its beautifully stacked stitches. A single crochet edging is crocheted last to finish off the design.

title: Wildflowers Ba
description: The Wildflowers Baby Blanket is made with the beautiful colors and exquisite softness of Stroll Tonal yarn. Colorful flower motifs with a cheerful dance of golden shells along the edging make this a one-of-a-kind blanket for a special someone. The smaller square blanket uses 36 motifs and the larger blanket uses 72. After whip-stitching the motifs together, three rounds of edging are worked: a round of SC, then a round of golden shells, and lastly a round of slanted shells for a delicate sawtooth outline.

title: Winter Cable Hat
description: ntertwined Cables twist around this cozy hat, designed to keep your noggin warm while you snowshoe through the woods on a crisp winter day. This hat begins with a ribbed band, worked sideways in a long strip, then joined to create a tube. The body of the hat is worked in the round from the ribbing to the top of the hat. The cables are easier than they look, just pay attention to whether your post stitches are worked in front or behind your previous stitches - this will ensure that they cross over each other correctly.

title: Winter Diamonds Market Bag
description: The design of this ultra-useful hobo-style market bag is inspired by colorful weavings from Mexico and the southwest US. The handles are just the right length for the bag to feel comfortable on your shoulder or in your hand. Choose your favorite colors to make your own bespoke one-of-a-kind bag. The bag is made from 13 identical motifs which are then crocheted together on the right side to create a decorative ridge seam between motifs. The handles and fastening tab are made amigurumi-style, as tubes, for added strength. A magnetic snap is concealed under the button on the fastening tab for extra security.

title: Amigurumi Stitch! from Lilo and Stitch
description:
pattern corrections:
inner ear and outer ear...interchange it :D Lately, I've been getting requests to make an amigurumi stitch. At first, I was hesitant because of the phenomena that is stitch and also because I've seen a couple of really good ones already. But then, I decided that in order for me to further improve myself in this craft, I have to be able to skillfully and artistically recreate characters that are hyped. After days of sketching out the design [and having the picture of stitch be the only thing i look at on my laptop] and another few days of crocheting it, I ended up with this little stitch. And, I am proud to say that despite the amount of time I put into this, I am more than proud to say that this is my pattern! I hope that you will enjoy making your own stitch as well with this pattern.
"Ohana means family, and family means that nobody gets left behind or forgotten"
Also, feel free to check out my other designs :D i'll be putting up a new mini ebook by n

title: Leaf Garland
description: This pattern is a great one to have in your library, because from the same basic design, you can work a great variety of things! Details on how to vary the basic pattern are included. You can make a lovely long decorative garland in colours to suit the season to decorate your mantle or a gift. Work short lengths to make napkin rings. You can work in a thick woollen yarn to make a warm leafy scarf, or with fine cotton or cobweb yarn to make a necklace with an organic vibe. Use metallic yarn for a Christmas garland, or household string for an unusual washing line! There really are endless permutations! The pattern is fully illustrated with colour photos, making it suitable for beginners branching out from the basics. Available in both UK and US crochet terminology - both files are included, just choose whichever you prefer to use. All my patterns also come with my e-mail support if you have any questions. title: No Sew Mushroom Pop
description: Meet Molly the mushroom crochet pop! This little fidget is not only adorable, but super fun to pop. They are great to sell, gift, or even just keep all to yourself ;). Molly is completely no sew - except for sewing up at the end (which you can also do with a crochet hook). This pattern is written in US crochet terms only. I recommend you use a stitch marker as this is crocheted in continuous rounds. Techniques Used: The use of no-sew methods and embroidery for details indicates a need for some crochet experience. Stitch Variety: The inclusion of special stitches like the bobble stitch and working in front or back loops only suggests a level above beginner. However, special stitches are described and shown in photos, making this pattern great for beginners looking to expand their crochet skills. Molly is apart of a bigger bundle that is available on my Etsy shop (link in bio), which includes more photos, Molly the mushroom, a chonky mushroom boi, and Steve the strawberry (all pops).

title: Basic Granny Square
description: This written crochet pattern (in US terms) is my version of a simple granny square (others may have slight variations in some of the steps, but the final product usually works out the same). A granny square can be used for making little squares (or larger ones – simply continue the pattern for as many rounds as desired) to sew together for blankets, cushion covers, shawls, or scarves. Use any number of colours, in any number of combinations. Any yarn (with crochet hook to match yarn weight) can be used. Gauge is not important for this pattern. The only additional material required is a darning needle, with which to weave in ends.

title: Any-Size Square Container
description: An easy-to-make container with a square base which can be made in any width, height or number of colours, making it a good project for beginners or for using up yarn left over from other projects. The container is worked in rounds from the base up and can be made with or without the handles. This pattern uses both American and British crochet terms. You will need: Although you can use any yarn, for a robust container I recommend using a thick yarn (aran or thicker) in a non-stretchy fibre such as raffia, cotton or jute. I used Bobbiny 5mm recycled cotton cord in Black and Natural. The smallest-sized crochet hook that you can comfortably use with your chosen yarn. The smaller your hook the denser your stitches will be and the better your container will hold its shape. I used an 8mm (US L/11) hook. Stitch markers. A tapestry needle if using yarn, or glue if using thick cord.

title: Star Dreamcatcher
description: This is a quick and easy project that makes a great gift. Crochet the small mandala of 8 rounds with a little bit double-sided texture. That means that we work in this mandala on the Right Side (as ususal) and we work one round on the Wrong Side. This is a way to get a texture on both sides of the mandala. Decorate the dreamcatcher with whatever you like, such as wooden or gemstones beads, shells, coloured ribbon or self-made tassels. title: Stitched Up Make Up Scrubbies
description: This make up scrubbies pattern includes instructions for two sizes, one approx. 6.5cm/2.5" for a small scrubbie and 9.5cm/3.5" for a larger scrubbie. It is ideal for a beginner or confident beginner, with plenty of step by step photos. Stitches used are - (US) SS, CH, SC, Puff Stitch (incudes a written instruction) and Magic Circle. (UK) SS, CH, DC, Puff Stitch (incudes a written instruction) and Magic Circle. I have used Drops Paris Aran weight yarn for my scrubbies, with a 4.5mm hook but you could use any Aran/Worsted Cotton yarn to achieve a similar result. You could also use a different weight yarn, just use a half size smaller hook than that recommended by your yarn to ensure it is tight enough. If you have any questions please contact me, I am more than happy to help (both before and after pattern purchase!). title: Oceana Bikini
description: This bikini top puts a twist on the traditional string bikini, making it perfect for relaxing by the pool or chilling at the beach. Like a traditional string bikini this top has cups that move, meaning you can get the fit and coverage you desire. A combination of shell stitches and chaining creates the scalloped edging. This pattern is written for cup sizes: Small, Medium and Large with a totally customisable back tie. However information is given on how to adjust the cups sizes for the perfect fit. Pattern uses US terms. Materials used: 1 Skein of Wendy Supreme Cotton DK (DK,100% Cotton, 201m per 100g) or substitute cotton yarn 3mm hook Yarn needle Stitch Markers (optional) Knitting-in elastic (optional) Cup Sizes: Small – base of the cup should measure 6.5” (16.5cm) Medium – base of the cup should measure 7.5” (19cm) Large – Base of the cup should measure 8.5” (21.6cm) Back Tie: Can be made as long as required.

description: This crochet pattern for the Caterpillar by Sarah Quillen creates a soft lovable 13" stuffed animal. The terms are US. title: Grimm the Ghost
description: This little guy likes to think he is spooky, but we can all see he is just a cutie! Grimm is about 4.5 inches tall (11.5 cm) when using worsted/aran weight yarn, so he is great for using up some stash yarn you have lying around. This free ghost amigurumi pattern includes detailed instructions and step-by-step photos, plus you can contact me along the way to ask any questions. It is an easy level pattern that involves starting with a magic ring, slip stitch, single crochet, half double crochet, increasing, decreasing, attaching pieces, and working through both loops, front loop only, and back loop only. Materials you will need: E US (3.5 mm) hook worsted weight yarn, approximately 45 yrds (41 m) yarn/tapestry needle removable stitch markers two 15 mm plastic safety eyes polyester fiberfill stuffing Note: If you are making this amigurumi for someone under 3 yrs, I recommend crocheting or embroidering the eyes instead of using the plastic safety eyes.

title: Margo Barbie Jumper
description: The Margo Barbie Jumper is inspired by the jumper Margot Robbie wore for her 'Barbie' tour in Australia. This turtle neck would be a perfect gift for your loved ones. And hey the best part? It can also be a unisex jumper excluding the ribbon. The jumper has a 5" ease but you are free to add more ease if you are looking for an oversized fit. It is a made-to-measure pattern so it can fit on any body type and size. You also don't have to match any gauge which is a plus point. You can grab any yarn you have ranging from sports weight yarn upto dk worsted weight yarn and a suitable crochet hook to get started on your own jumper. title: Granny Square Fabric Dress
description: This tutorial was made in collaboration with La Tessoria! Tutorial is written for two sizes, but it is extremely easy to make it in any other size. All you need to do is to make as many squares as needed to make it fit around child(tm)s chest. And then cut the fabric according to your own measurements ” desired length and width of the skirt part. Materials: any sport weight yarn, approx. 50g for smaller size and up to 100g for bigger sizes (I used linen/viscose blend yarn, but any other yarn will work just fine. With the yarn weight you can also make experiments and use some thicker yarn as well. In this case you will simply have to make less squares crochet hook 3.25mm (D) or size needed to obtain gauge 4 small buttons (can use only 2 if prefer) tapestry needle fabric (two pieces each 15x18inches for baby size or 18x21inches for toddler size 4 stitch markers (optional) pins (optional) sewing machine (optional, can hand sew seams instead) A suggested yarn is shown below.

title: Balls of (almost) Any Size
description: After making a lot of amigurumi items that had ball shapes in them, I realised that there was a simple mathematical way of working out how to get whatever size ball I was trying to make, more or less, knowing how many rows were needed between the shaping for the top and bottom of the ball (sphere). The first eight sizes are here in full, after that I tell you how to continue if you want to make larger ones. Don’t worry, you won’t have to do more than count and maybe divide an even number in half! So here you have a pattern you can use to make a huge number of different sizes of ball or sphere with whatever yarn you have to hand – although I do recommend a firm one, such as a cotton, if you want a fairly accurate sphere. It’s also a good way to use up leftovers – I include how to work out how big a ball you can make with the yarn you have, although you WILL need to do some maths for that.

title: Give it a Whirl Hat
description: The Give it a Whirl Hat pattern was inspired by the knitted Cute Snail Hat by Omura. When I designed this hat, I'd not yet learned to knit, so I resorted to creating my own crochet version to achieve a similar look. The Give it a Whirl hat is incredibly easy and works up quickly, so it's great for batch gifting! Pattern includes specific instructions to make an adult size hat, as well as information on adjusting for smaller or larger hat size.  Keep in mind that adjusting the size will change yarn requirements. The sample was worked in Bernat Mosaic (now discontinued). A substitute yarn is shown below.

Have fun and be creative.

title: Sunbeams Granny Square
description: The Sunbeams Granny Square uses front post double crochet stitches emanating from the centre of the square to symbolise the sun's rays. It is a happy square which can be used to make lots of crochet projects. It is an advanced beginner pattern and uses single, half double, double and treble crochet stitches, together with front post double crochet stitches. All stitches are explained in detail in the pattern notes with photo tutorials. Pattern in US terminology. The dimensions are 10x10cm/4"x4", using fingering weight/4 ply yarn (18-25m in total) and a 3.0mm hook. I changed yarn colour every round requiring 7 colours. Other weight yarn could be used.

description: Very easy pattern for a cute summer tank top and it is very quick to make! It is crocheted in one long piece and then buttoned together in the front. The strap ties are made separately and sewed on at the end. The pattern is easy to adjust for all sizes including kids and plus sizes! I show directions for chest sizes 32” to 45 1/2” but again it is really easy to adjust it. The finished length is 9” for all sizes but it can be made longer or shorter. The pattern uses basic American crochet terminology and if you have experience with reading a written pattern this is a great pattern for a beginner. title: Bouquet Bucket Hat
description: Easily modifiable - Some guidance is given for modification. Pattern is written with the expectation that you know the following: Working in the round Back loop only crochet Double Crochet Chain Half Double Crochet Fasten Off Magic Ring Increases Finished hat based on the pattern is considered "medium, adult" This pattern is part of my market makes series! The idea for this hat came from the increases on a granny square blanket I was working on. I wanted a bucket hat that looked like the granny square, but I wound up making a ruffle hat instead! This is a pattern for a standard size bucket hat. Instructions are not specific to sizing, and sizing can be increased or decreased based on the crown size. Guidance is given during increases and creation of the cap portion to maintain stitch count, but the ruffle portion may be applied to any size hat! This pattern would be wonderful for markets that are gift-centric or interactive.

title: Spiraling Crochet Preemie Hat
description: This pattern provides instructions in US-terms for a crochet preemie hat in two most common sizes. It is ideal for using even the smallest left-overs - avoid weaving in ends by crocheting over them as you go! The hat is worked in half-double crochet. For the spiraling effect, you insert the hook not into the standard loops at the top of the stitch, but into the back loop and the third loop that is formed at the back of each stitch. sizes head circumference 31(33.5)cm / 12.25(13.25) inch finished circumference 28(30)cm / 11(11.75) inch In the spirit of charity, this pattern will be available for free. If you would like to thank me, consider purchasing one of my other designs, for yourself or for a friend!

title: Maestrale
description: Maestrale is a triangular shawl with an airy lace pattern and a little hint of texture. It is perfect for summer, to wear on a plain dress or even to be used as a pareo on the beach! This pattern was designed with Scheepjes Whirl, but you are free to use your favorite yarn, of course. I would recommend using fingering weight yarn. Using a different weight could or will require adjustments that I cannot provide in this pattern. Feel free to mix and match your favorite colors and your favorite yarn, use your own creativity! Please, take your time to check your gauge. You can crochet the first 10 rows and measure one whole repeat. It should be a square 8 x 8 cm (about 3 x 3 inch). This way you can use all your Scheepjes Whirl cake. You can choose to ignore the gauge requirements if you are using a different yarn, of course. I hope you enjoy as I did! For more information, see: https://nimhriel.wordpress.com/2017/06/14/maestrale/

title: Flopsy and Mopsy
description: Hi all! The Flopsy and Mopsy Bunnies are my free Easter pattern offering (although these cuties are perfect for any day)! They are inspired by an earlier set of pom-pom bunnies I made, however, I wanted to try and recreate that fluffy texture in crochet. Although the pattern itself is very simple, this fuzzy yarn can be a little intimidating to crochet with at first. Just take it slow and with a little practice, it's not too hard to pick up! I hope you all enjoy making Flopsy and Mopsy, and as always I would love to see your projects so #lovenstitchdesigns on Instagram or feel free to shoot me an email or review if you have any questions or feedback! Thank you!! P.S. This pattern uses US terminology.

title: Japanese Knot Project Bag
description: This is a quick and easy bag to make.  It's perfect for your small, take-along projects.  The bag is 7 inches (17.8cm) high, and the square bottom is 5 inches x 5 inches (12.7cm square).    To close the bag, simply push the longer strap through the loop made by the shorter strap.  It can, then, be worn on your wrist.  Gauge is not really important; however, you will see that I used a smaller hook size in order to create a dense fabric for the bag. Stitches used--chain, single crochet, double crochet, triple crochet, slip stitch.

title: Gemerald Tee
description: The Gemerald Tee is a vibrant crochet cropped T-shirt that is perfect for adding a pop of color to your wardrobe. This project utilizes the “linked double crochet” stitch, which I think you’re gonna love. It’s thick with drape and doesn’t take long to work-up! The top is worked from the top down, allowing for customizable length and eliminating the need for seaming.

title: The Barryville Top
description: The Barryville top is a cropped tank made for high waisted shorts or jeans. Worked from the bottom up in joined rows to create clean lines of color throughout. The top is worked last and the straps could be modified to be longer/shorter to suit size and preference. A great top for summer and named after Barryville, a Hamlet in upstate NY, which happens to be my favorite camping locale. This pattern is only available in size medium for now. I made this pattern to go with Hobby Lobby’s Yarn Bee Pigment + Fiber. The top shown is made in the colorway called “Fridays in Florence” but you can choose any colorway/Combination you like. I have labeled the yarn colors as A,B,C,D,E,F,G. I hope you enjoy it! :) Size Medium fits Bust sizes 34"-36".

title: Sakura Granny Square
description: This easy to make, textured granny square with a flower motif in the centre is inspired by my other design, the Sakura Face Scrubbies. The pattern is using front post and back post stitches to create a nice, textured look. Finished size of the square is approx. 5”x 5”, which can be used to make a big, granny square blanket. I used DK yarn, but you can use any yarn and use as many colours as you want US terms used throughout I hope you’ll enjoy this little project! Happy crocheting! Krisztina

title: Beach Bag
description: Make this colourful beach bag with this easy to follow crochet pattern. The pattern is available in US terms. In the pattern I explain how to make this bag and how to make it bigger or smaller. For this bag you need to know how to make a: chain stitch, slip stitch, single crochet and a double crochet. This bag doesn't need a lining but of course you can add one if you like. I used Phildar Cotton 3 and a crochet hook size 3mm for this pattern. The pattern contains my email address just in case you need help during the making of this pattern. Please don't hesitate to contact me if you need help, I'm happy to help if necessary.

title: Plush Pumpkins
description: Have you seen those adorable plush pumpkins that are all the rage? Want to make one . . . or two . . . or ten for your own collection? Then you need this pattern! You will work with basic stitches including chain, single crochet, and half double crochet. You'll create the pumpkin's ridges by working the in back loop, and finish off by stuffing and adding embellishments. You'll also be provided options to make the pumpkin in different sizes. After all, these are like potato chips - you won't be able to make just one! title: Three Little Bowls
description: I wrote this pattern about 5 years ago when I was teaching a day class for the Surrey Women’s Institute. Since then, I have often used it for 2 hour “taster” classes. Don’t worry – it won’t take you 2 hours to make one of these little bowls! I have made the smaller one in less than 30 minutes. It’s a nice pattern for beginners but also a quick, fun and satisfying little project for more experienced crocheters that returns a utilitarian result. I was prompted to publish this pattern when I taught a private class to three ladies at a clients house. This time, I had experimented with multiple strands of yarn and different hook sizes. Each bowl uses the exact same pattern but with a different number of strands of Aran yarn and subsequent hook size. I was so delighted with the different results you can get, simply by adding a strand of yarn and going up a hook size that I thought I’d share the pattern with you all.

title: Roma Lavinia Skirt
description: Roma Lavinia Skirt is made by combining knitting and sewing techniques. Apart from these 2 techniques, crocheting the drawstring for the waist is the only extra skill you will need. The skirt is completed in rounds, on circular needles, top down by holding 3 strands of yarn. The start is at the waist and with a lace border which will later be used for fastening – drawstring will be pulled through it and tied to fit your waist measurement. There are 5 sections of the skirt in the form of ruffles. Sections 1, 2, 4 and 5 are knitted, while Section 3 is a rectangular piece of fabric that is sewn onto Sections 2 and 4. Between each section there is a lace border knitted in 5 rounds. Sections 1 and 2 are knitted to fit your hip measure, and they tightly wrap your body around. Sections 3, 4 and 5 are wider than each section before, thus forming ruffles and flare. The pattern is written in US terminology and covers both centimeter and inch metric systems.

title: Toadstools & Mushrooms
description: These folksy toadstools and mushrooms look like something straight out of a fairytale. They are simple and easy to make, so perfect for those starting out with crochet. Once finished they make for fun novelty keepsakes, or look fabulous when threaded and turned into rustic hanging ornaments. Materials Light beige and dark brown yarn for the Mushrooms. Red and cream / white yarn for the Toadstools Crochet Hook Stitch marker Scissors Darning needle Stuffing to fill your Mushrooms / Toadstools Natural Twine to hang your Mushrooms / Toadstools For the Toadstools you need small amounts of Red and White (or cream) coloured yarn. The Mushrooms are made using a Light Beige and Dark Brown yarn. Make sure to use a smaller hooksize than you normally would use for the type of yarn that you choose. The stitches need to be tight enough to prevent the stuffing from showing through. We went with a Worsted Weight yarn and used a 2.5mm crochet hook. Happy Hooking!

title: Jam Roly Poly
description: The Jam Roly Poly is an iconic British steamed suet pudding, and a childhood favourite for many! This pudding dates back to the mid 19th century, why not give yours a modern twist by serving up with scoops of colourful Neopolitan ice cream! Finished size: Jam Roly Poly 8cm diameter, ice cream 4.5cm diameter. Materials: Paintbox Yarns Simply DK, 15g Vanilla Cream, 10g Red Wine, 5g Paper White, 5g Coffee Bean, 5g Candyfloss Pink; crochet hook size 3.5mm; stuffing; tapestry needle for sewing in ends; scissors. The pattern is written in standard UK crochet terminology and is suitable for beginners. The main stitch used for the pudding and ice cream is the double crochet, and you can find a tutorial for this stitch right here - https://www.lovecrafts.com/en-gb/how-to-double-crochet.

title: Montana Poncho
description: A great new twist on an old classic - the Poncho. The Montana Poncho features a slit side to make it functional and keep your arms accessible. That was something I always wanted to remain true to my designs: function AND fashion. Perfect for a cup of coffee on the porch on a brisk morning or carrying my bag to market. The turtleneck has textured crochet ribbing to give you all the cozy feels. It works up easy and is perfect for markets and errands and I even wear it around the house as a sort of blanket shirt. This poncho is worked from the top down.  First, the collar is crocheted sideways and seamed. The body of the poncho is worked down from the side of the collar. Instructions for Sizes:  Extra Small/Small, Medium/Large, 1X/2X, 3X/4X Finished Measurements:  ◦ Bust: 50.25 (54.75, 61.75, 66.25)”  ◦ Length: 26.25 (27, 27.75, 28.5)”

title: Reusable Produce Bag
description: The Reusable Produce Bag was created to provide an alternative to the single-use plastic bags common in supermarkets. The convenience of the nylon bags is undeniable and the momentum of their usage is huge, so we will have a hard time changing our ways, but for many of us making small steps for decreasing the usage of plastics and making more environmentally friendly choices is the way to go. Many options for reusable bags are available for sale already, but as a crocheter, I love the idea of making them myself. The bag is worked from the base up in the round using an easy mesh stitch. The pattern is written and charted, and includes three different sizes (S/M/L), based on the measurements of some commonly found reusable bags and drawstring produce pouches. Sizing is very easy to customize and adjust. First several rounds while shaping the base require attention, then it is a mindless and monotonous project.

title: Crossrows Earwarmer
description: If you loved my other earwarmer patterns, you will love this free pattern too. It’ll be part of a set (matching beanies and a cowl) so keep your eyes opened and check my site regularly for updates. Say hello to the Crossrows Earwarmer , perfect for the chilly but sunny autumn walks! The name of the design comes from the stitches I used to create the pattern and I was playing with the words to come up with a fun and easy-to-remember name. It’s a fun and easy pattern to use up some scrap aran/worsted yarn and it is easily adjustable to fit any size. Difficulty: Easy/Advanced Beginner Size: One size only to fit an average adult’s head, but it can be easily adjusted to make a bigger or smaller size. US terms used throughout I hope you will enjoy this pattern as much as I loved designing it. Happy crocheting! Krisztina

title: Square Barbie
description: How do you make a jacket from a square without cutting off any pieces? This pattern will show you how. You are allowed to make cuts into the shape, and join it back together but not take anything off. The pattern contains four easy outfits for Barbie (and other fashion dolls), which are basically much the same. They all produce similar results but the ways of getting there are different. Three are knitting, the other is crochet. If you just want to make simple, pretty, outfits for Barbie that’s fine. The pattern also includes a very basic, mini-tutorial for garment design, using squares and rectangles. It might make you look at shapes in a new way. It tells you how to scale-up Barbie’s jacket to fit a child or adult. It even shows you how a dishcloth shape can become a scarf.

Also, you can visit my website for tips, and you can always email me for help! title: Simple Snowflake Earrings
description:
A small simple snowflake to grace your ears, these snowflakes are only 3 rounds to make.  They only utilize chain, double crochet, and slip stitches, making it perfect for someone just beginning crocheting (try it with larger yarn first), and a great entry into thread crochet.  Each snowflake took less than 15 minutes. Also included in the pattern is a little photo tutorial on how to open the loops of the fish hook earrings if you've never worked with wire before. This pattern is fully charted and written out. It is written in US terms. Additional Information Materials:
-Size 20 Crochet Thread
-Size 10 Steel hook
-Tapestry needle
-2 fish hook earrings
-Blocking pins
-White glue
-Water
-Brush
-Pliers Abbreviations:
ch - chain
sl st - slip stitch
dc - double crochet
tch - turning chain
rep - repeat
sp(s) - space(s) Many thanks to 2skeins2go , Rufarocrafty , Amerz [Emylly123](/people/emyll

title: Learn to do Wiggly Crochet Hot Pad and Coaster
description: I designed this basic Wiggly Crochet hot pad and coaster pattern to go with my YouTube video. You can find me on YouTube as The Crochet Architect. I've been doing wiggly crochet for years and I wanted to share this wonderful crochet technique with other crocheters. In Wiggly Crochet, you make the foundation mesh first, then crochet the wiggly stitches on top. Wiggly Crochet projects are 3-dimensional with cushion in them! Wiggly Crochet makes great hot pads, coasters and rugs! I hope you enjoy the Wiggly Crochet technique and this free pattern!

title: Christmas Pickle Ornament
description:
What is a Christmas pickle? A Christmas pickle is a tradition of mysterious origins, although most likely American. According to tradition, a pickle is hung in the Christmas tree, and the first child to find it on
Christmas morning receives a special gift. Glass-blown pickle ornaments have become commonplace, but we think this crocheted one is just perfect! To complete this pattern, you will need:
* size H (5 mm) crochet hook
* green worsted weight yarn
* polyester stuffing This is an 'easy' crochet pattern that requires minimal shaping, and use of a bobble stitch (instructions provided)

title: Windowpane Scarf
description: The Windowpane is the perfect spring or fall scarf to keep the chill off your neck without being bulky. I really enjoy making this scarf, and I have to say I love the finished result each time. It is a really easy pattern that works up very quickly. The Windowpane was designed to showcase the colorshifts in gradient yarns like Knit Picks Chroma Fingering, or Noro Taiyo Sock, but you'll also get a beautiful scarf using solid, semi-solid, or variegated yarns. Pattern includes instructions for making your scarf wider or narrower, and suggested changes for working with heavier yarns. Just remember that changing yarns or working a wider scarf may increase your yarn requirements. Finished (but unblocked) dimensions: 67" long x 5-1/4" wide. This stitch pattern develops a slant toward one side, so a light blocking is definitely recommended.

title: Cherry Blossom
description: The pattern has 8 videos that guide in detail the weaving step by step, especially for crochet makers who start in the art of 3D weaving. The written pattern is 50 pages with 90 images. Cherry Blossom is a square, made with 3D technique, that can serve as the cushion, a blanket or work several in different colors and join them. The size after blocking is 15,7”X15,7”/40x40 cm. The model is made with Stylecraft Special and Batik yarn. It can be made in Scheepajes Colorcrafter since both threads have the same elasticity and gauge. Other yarns with similar characteristics are Studio Essential by Deramores, Astra by Patons, Elle Family Knit DK and Emu Clasic DK. If you want to work with cotton, Muskad and Safron of the brands Drops is a good option, however, the elasticity is slightly lower, so –depending on your tension– sometimes you will have to add one or two chains Happy knitting!

title: Chocolate Eclair
description: Choux pastry is a staple of good patisserie baking, and the classic eclair is filled with whipped cream and topped with chocolate icing. But no need to worry about the correct oven temperature with these crochet bakes! Finished size: 15cm x 5cm x 5cm. Materials: Paintbox Yarns Simply DK, 15g Light Caramel, 10g Champagne White, 5g Coffee Bean; crochet hook size 3.5mm; stuffing; tapestry needle for sewing in ends; scissors. The pattern is written in standard UK crochet terminology and is suitable for beginners. The main stitch used for the eclair is the half treble crochet. You can find a tutorial for this stitch in the Crochet How-To Techniques section of the LoveCrafts website.

title: Minion and Evil Minion
description:
This pattern does not include the fire hat - however if you are interested I will message you the link to the free pattern. Two patterns in One! Your choice to make an evil minion or regular minion or a one of each. Each minion measures appox. 5 1/2 inches tall when completed (a little more if you include the hair). This is a 9 page pdf file with many helpful pictures to help you better understand the pattern. Including pictures on how to make the evil minions hair, teeth, and other things. For the Evil Minion You Will Need… ● Craftsmart Yarn in Purple (Main Body Color)
● Red Heart Super Saver in Black
● Small amount of Red Heart Super Saver in Orchid
● Small amount of Red Heart Super Saver in White
● Small amount of Red Heart Super Saver in Grey
● Puffy Fabric paint in Black and Light Blue (optional)
● If you do not paint your eyes you can also glue on a googly eye. For the Yellow Minion You Will Need… ● Red Heart Super Saver in Bri

title: Bucket hat - with a straight brim

title: Superbly Simple Baby Blanket
description: This simple crochet baby blanket is an easy to follow pattern great for beginning crocheters to use for their very first crocheted afghans. Worked in double knit this lightweight blanket is easy to memorise making it a perfect pattern for relaxing hooky time! However you may use any yarn type and hook size of your choice with this pattern (it’s that simple) so no need to worry about meeting a specific gauge. Make it a baby sized blanket, as shown, or go as big as you like by simply adding more rounds! The addition of a simple border finishes off the blanket nicely. This pattern is my new favourite go-to blanket; as it’s an easy pattern it works up quickly - what more could you ask for!? There are many options for how you could arrange colours on this blanket. A fab stash buster for using up odds and ends.

title: Ocean Breeze Kimono
description: The Ocean Breeze Kimono is made from shell stitches, which are worked with 3 easy crochet stitches: chain stitch, single and double crochet. After two set up rows, you will work a repetition of only two basic rows. Those are easy to remember and make the pattern fun to work! Additional gauge information : 10 x 10 cm are received with 11 stitches (of the initial chain stitch row, which translates into 3 shells and 3 single crochet) and 7 rows. You should use a light yarn , because the shell stitches require a lot of yarn which can result in a very heavy garment.

title: Butterfly Pocket Wrap for Preemies
description: I designed this pattern for the charity Upon Butterfly Wings. You can find Lucy's website on Google, and you can also find Upon Butterfly Wings on both Facebook and Instagram. This is a Pocket Wrap, which forms a cocoon type blanket for babies born too soon. The blanket/wrap is designed to be made between 6 inches and 12 inches in order to fit premature babies. Of course, you can alter the pattern to make it any size you wish. Instructions are given in UK Terms, and if you do use my pattern, I would love for you to tag me on Facebook (Karen Pimblett) and on Instagram (@--Kernowdesigns) so that I can see what you have created. :0) Please do not sell, copy or redistribute my pattern. title: Uplifter
description: This easy pattern designed for one cake of self-striping yarn makes a lovely gift for people of all ages and genders. Wear it knotted like a scarf or draped like a wrap. It's a beginner-friendly project with a relaxing two-row repeat. Requires only chain and double crochet stitches in US terms. Yarn: about 590 yards (540 meters) of dk weight yarn (light, category 3). The sample scarf used 1 cake of Lion Brand Mandala yarn (100% acrylic; 590 yards (450 m) per 150-gram cake) in the colorway Mothra. You can use any yarn you like. Use the hook size recommended on the label. You can use scraps if you prefer. Go with the flow of color changes- no cutting or controlling of colors needed. Share your scarf with #uplifterscarf on Instagram! Please feel free to sell finished items made from this pattern as long as you give design credit as “Uplifter crochet pattern by Pretty Peaceful”. Thank you for your support.

title: Slipper Socks
description: Classic Slipper Socks that are great for around the house, under boots, and for those cold nights. Worked in Tunisian Crochet . They are made from the Cuff to the Toe, all worked in-the-round apart from the Heel Flap which is worked straight. Slip Stitch decreases are used for the Gusset shaping, and Mitered decreases are used for the Toe shaping. The Tunisian Knit Stitch is used for the Main body of the Socks, with the Tunisian Simple Stitch and Twisted Tunisian Simple Stitch used for the Ribbing on the Cuff. MATERIALS: 5mm Double-ended Tunisian Crochet Hook, 1 Main Stitch Marker to mark the beginning of each round, 2 Additional Stitch Markers when working the Gusset, 95 (120, 150)yards of DK per sock for the Forward Pass yarn, 95 (130, 165)yards of DK per sock for the Return yarn TECHNIQUES: Working Tunisian Crochet In The Round, Slip Stitch Decreasing, Mitered Decreasing (3 Tunisian Simple stitches worked together)

title: Crochet Scarf
description: This is a pattern for a lovely scarf. Once finished the scarf measures 9" x 96". You can use any yarn suitable for a crochet hook size 4.5mm or 5mm. In this pattern you will find a clear photo tutorial on how to make the stitches that I used for this pattern. As the tutorial is very easy to follow, this pattern is suitable for a beginner. Just make sure you know how to make a chain stitch and a double crochet (US terms). I used 1200 meters (1312 yards) of yarn for a shawl that is approximately 23cm (9”) wide and 244cm(96”) long. Please note that all my patterns are written in US crochet terms.

title: Cover any size Jar or Pot
description: This is a method to cover any size of straight-sided jar or pot with a lid that screws on or similar (I have some with a sort of ‘bung’ lid). You work the base first, to fit the size, then go up the side, then decrease to keep it in place (IF the jar/pot has a ‘shoulder’), and do the same the other way up to make the cover for the lid. It’s really easy, and the results make good storage pots and nice little gifts, especially if you have small items to go inside such as jewellery or beauty products, or small toys such as marbles or game pieces – even cookies. Most yarns, including the cottons I like to use for this, will stretch enough that the covers can be put on and taken off as often as you like if they or the jars need washing! I also suggest how to work out how much yarn you’ll need, but you do have to do at least part of the work for size, first. There's a simple version for a mustard jar at the end, to make it really clear what to do.

title: Tunisian Mask Mates / Ear Savers
description: A mask mate or an ear saver is an accessory that can be used with a face mask to prevent sore ears. Prolonged use of a face mask with elastic bands that go over the ears can make the ears very sore. There are a lot of health care workers who could benefit from using these mask mates because they have to wear such masks on a daily basis for long hours. **1. They prevent ears from getting sore because of prolonged use of masks.****2. They help people with small heads because the mask fits better with them.****3. They are made of cotton so they can be washed in hot water and disinfected.****4. They need a tiny amount of yarn so they can be made out of scraps.** 5. If you haven’t tried Tunisian crochet, this is a great pattern for beginners :)

title: Country Comfort Mittens
description: The Country Comfort Mitten Pattern gives you a very warm and super comfy pair of chunky mittens using Loops and Threads Country Loom yarn or Lion Brand Wool-Ease Thick & Quick yarn. This pattern will require one full skein of Country Loom yarn and a J (6.00mm) Hook. To get correct sizing, the gauge using the J Hook is as follows: in a 2”x2” stitch area you should have approximately 5.5 sc stitches and 5 rows. Adjust Hook size to meet proper gauge. NOTE: you can use Lion Brand Wool-Ease Thick & Quick yarn in this pattern, you’ll need 2 balls of yarn and a K Hook to get the same sizing. The gauge you’ll need to meet when using this yarn is within a 2”x2” area, you should get 5 sc’s across & 6 rows. The instructions outline making right and left mittens and give some tips on how to either add length or shorten the mittens for a more personalized fit. The finished mittens measure approximately 9” from top of the Mitten to the bottom of cuff and they measure approximately 4” across.

title: Gradual Beginner Wrap
description: The Gradual Beginner Wrap was designed to teach makers the basics of crochet! It includes the four basic stitches (chain, single crochet, half double crochet, and double crochet), and a variation on each of those stitches. The pattern includes a standard crochet pattern, a full photo tutorial (both right and left handed versions are included), as well as a descriptive written pattern perfect for beginners. This pattern was inspired by my friend Penny Stricklin (part of the wife-and-wife team at Cesium Yarn) and her knit design, the Gradual Beginner Shawl. Note: This PDF is extremely long, be careful if you decide to print! Pattern is written in US crochet terms. Yarn: DK weight (weight 3). Approximately 685 yards. I used approximately 600 yards of Cesium Yarn Merino DK in the color Fuchsia Flower. Note that tester yardage varied widely (as low as 470 yards, and as high as 760 yards) - having extra yarn available is never a bad thing!

title: Laid Back Lace Wrap
description: The Laid Back Lace Wrap is the simplest way to showcase the beautiful colorways of Lion Brand Mandala yarn. Sometimes the simplest projects are the best! Get into the groove and let your hook fly as the colors flow by. Requires only one cake of yarn with no cutting of color changes. Go with the flow! This simple, light rectangular wrap can be worn as a shawl or knotted like a scarf. Perfect as a first crochet wrap for a beginner, for a fast project for a gift, or as a project you can work on while you watch TV without having to think too much.  A fast crocheter could complete this wrap in less than 3 hours.  A more leisurely stitcher will require 6-7 hours. This is a beginner pattern that requires only chain and single crochet (US terms) stitches.  Finished dimensions are 168 by 51 cm after blocking. Please feel free to sell finished items made from this pattern if you give pattern design credit as "Laid Back Lace Wrap Pattern by Susan Kennedy of PrettyPeaceful".

title: Basic Walker Bag
description: I worked up this Basic Walker Bag after my grandmother had knee surgery and needed to use a walker. She needed something simple yet strong. Voila! Done! This bag would be a GREAT CHARITY PROJECT. It would look great for both men and women. It's simple, quick and works. You need an H hook and approx. 2 skeins of RH or ILTY. Any heavy worsted will work fine but you may need more depending on how big the skeins are. This bag is worked in one piece including the edging and straps. I hate weaving in ends so I am sure you don't like it either. Please do not copy, alter or redistribute this pattern for profit or claim it as your own. It is meant to be a charity pattern and I would like to keep it that way. You may sell what you make from it but I would love for you to GIVE some away as well.

title: Happy Waves Blanket
description: This Happy Waves Blanket is a great beginners pattern. In this pattern I'll explain how to make the blanket in the size you want and how to make the edging. You can use the edging tutorial on all future blanket projects. To make this pattern you need to know the basic stitches: chain, single crochet, half double crochet, double crochet, treble crochet (US terms). If you need help during the making of this pattern, you can contact me through the email address provided in the pattern. I made this blanket with Scheepjes Cotton Eight, but Phildar Coton 3 is a lovely alternative yarn. The Phildar Coton 3 colors that I picked for this blanket are similar to the Scheepjes-yarn colors that I used. The amount of yarn that you see here is just a basis starter pack that you can use to make a baby blanket. For a larger blanket you might need more yarn. The pattern is written in US terms. I used a crochet hook size 3mm.

title: Bear Spirit
description: Bear spirit was designed by Rosalyn Ducharme who purchased my Spiritual Flow pattern and created this strip as an addition for everyone to be able to use! NOTE this is only one strip, the bear pattern. For the Spiritual Flow pattern please see my Ravelry store. It’s an overlay mosaic crochet pattern using American terminology. The pattern has full written instructions and chart. The written instructions are based on repeating the design 3 times as with Spiritual Flow but you can easily do the pattern as you wish. Double knit yarn is usually used with this technique but you can use any yarn. A big thanks to Rosalyn Ducharme for this fantastic pattern! Enjoy! Sarah x

title: Lush Garden Bavarian Stitch Afghan
description: There is no gauge listed for the afghan, because it isn't required. There is no yardage listed for the yarn because I used all scraps and leftover balls to make this afghan. The only thing that I know for certain is that you will need at least one full skein of white to do the edging and joining. Final size is 72" x 52"- the squares themselves are 12"x12". All of the instructions are included for what I did to get my afghan to look like this, other than the Bavarian Stitch instructions. I feel like I need to let you know that this is NOT a beginner project. If you are not a strong crocheter, you will become discouraged by this pattern. For your sanity, please just be aware of that, because I won't be able to guide you through learning this pattern step-by-step.

title: Chevron Infinity Scarf
description: The scarf is worked in Tunisian Chevron Lace stitch pattern. It is basically a Tunisian Simple Stitch with increases and decreases to create the chevron/ripple stitch pattern. The sample in the picture has been worked in a brushed acrylic yarn on a much larger hook than the size suggested on the ball band to make the fabric more open and give the scarf some drape. The scarf can be made in any yarn. If you are using a finer yarn you will probably need more stitches. The pattern is an 8 stitch repeat. The scarf can also be made any length depending on how much yarn you have. The sample in the picture took all of one ball of the stated yarn.

title: Indian Spring
description: The pattern has 7 videos that guide in detail the weaving step by step, especially for crochet makers who start in the art of 3D weaving. The written pattern has 40 pages 40 with 65 images. Indian Spring is a square, made with 3D technique, that can serve as the center of a cushion, a blanket or work several in different colors and join them. The size after blocking is 10”X10”/33x33 cm. The model is made with Sheepjes Colour Crafter yarn. It can be made in Stylecraft Special Dk since both threads have the same elasticity and gauge. Other yarns with similar characteristics are Studio Essential by Deramores, Astra by Patons, Elle Family Knit DK and Emu Clasic DK. If you want to work with cotton, Muskad and Safron of the brands Drops is a good option, however, the elasticity is slightly lower, so –depending on your tension– sometimes you will have to add one or two chains Happy knitting!

title: Baby Seal Sarah
description: Baby Seal Sarah is part of Project Arctic. In Project Arctic I crochet different animals that live on or around the poles. They may live on the ice, in the air or in the water. What makes those animals special, is that many of them have a very different appearance when they're young compared to when they are grown up. And that is very nice to crochet! Size The seal will be about 20 cm long and 10 cm wide. title: Puffpearls Cord & Guide to Jewelry Basics
description: My [Crochet Inspirations Newsletter #47][1] goes into more detail about the rayon threads used. I've pulled together an easy "Puffpearls" cord stitch and a versatile "Mushroom Button" & loop crochet clasp to create a cord pattern + reference guide to some basics of jewelry crochet . The reference guide features include: A table of standard and traditional necklace lengths (in both inches & cm) Troubleshooting tips for pairing different types of pendants with crochet cords How to know which type of beginning slip know you make and why it matters The "best practices" that I've come to recognize as unique to jewelry crochet. We want our crochet jewelry to look fabulous, to last a long time, and to stay put (not shift around, not loosen, and certainly not fall off!).
