title: Cluster Pops
description: Designing a 6" square is hard for me. So what do I do, volunteer to make one LOL I was up for the challenge of something small for a change. Cluster Pops was born from a 12" called No Pops square a couple years ago. I did the opposite of No Pops. This is a fun little square with a new stitch, of course. I like to give people a new stitch to play with :wink: I am trying out a new layout for my patterns and I hope you like it. Don't worry it's nothing odd, just different from what I have been doing. I think it's easier to follow, but you can decide for yourself. Please let me know what you think. Also, I told you small was hard for me LOL I had to grow it out to 12". The larger version will be released soon. The 6" is just a taste and let's you get familiar with my writing style.

title: Baby Blue Whale Vincent
description: Blue Whale Baby Vincent is part of Project Arctic. In Project Arctic I crochet different animals that live on or around the poles. They may live on the ice, in the air or in the water. What makes those animals special, is that many of them have a very different appearance when they’re young compared to when they are grown up. And that is very nice to crochet! Blue whales are the largest animals on the planet. They can grow to a staggering 30m and weigh up to 160 tonnes. That is similar to a jumbojet! These whales cross our oceans and come North for their favorite food: krill.

title: Arc-en-ciel après l'orage
description: Arc-en-ciel après l’orage (rainbow after the storm) I made this blanket for a friend who loves anything multicoloured. Since it was a surprise and because she wouldn’t know the baby’s gender ahead of time, I based the design on what I knew of her preferences: lots of colours, natural fibres and above all, no white or off-white. She’d already told me she liked granny squares, and everything added up to become this Rainbow after the storm blanket.  Material  Hook: 5 mm  Yarn: Wool of the Andes Worsted (4) from KnitPicks, in the charted colours. 1 skein of each will be plenty, only the grey will require 3 skeins.

title: Victoria Sponge Cake
description: Is there a cake more classic than the humble Victoria Sponge?! With this simple crochet pattern you can make a sumptuous slice of the quintessential sponge cake, filled with buttercream and jam! Finished size: the cake slice measures 8cm wide x 10cm long x 6cm high. Materials: Paintbox Yarns Simply DK, 15g Vanilla Cream (for sponge cake), 10g Champagne White (for icing and buttercream), 5g Red Wine (for jam); crochet hook size 3.5mm; stuffing; tapestry needle for sewing in ends; scissors. The pattern is written in standard UK crochet terminology and is suitable for beginners, with the top and base worked back and forth in rows and the sides worked as rounds. The main stitch used is the half treble crochet, and you can find a tutorial for this stitch right here - https://www.lovecrafts.com/en-gb/c/article/how-to-half-double-crochet

title: Tummy Time Nursery Rug
description: When we found out we were expecting a baby (!), one of the first things I thought to make was a super squishy crocheted rug for the nursery. It needed to fit a few criteria: no holes so little fingers won't get caught and little toes won't trip, a flat circle, really soft and squishy for tummy time, and a cute border that also doesn't have any holes. I searched and searched, but I couldn't find the perfect pattern. So I decided to free-wheel it! This "pattern" is very flexible and can be changed up depending on how big you want your rug and how tight/loose you crochet, but this will give you the specifics on how I got my perfect rug and general guidelines on how you can make yours. All you need to know are single crochet, slip stitch, and how to make a magic circle, and you'll be on your way!
final_description: Tummy Time Nursery Rug Independent Designer Crochet The Ivy Killers Crochet English 1.0patterns nanpages Intermediate Super BulkySee Super Bulky Knitting Patterns Knitting PatternsSee Super Bulky Crochet PatternsSee Super Bulky Yarns When we found out we were expecting a baby (!), one of the first things I thought to make was a super squishy crocheted rug for the nursery. It needed to fit a few criteria: no holes so little fingers won't get caught and little toes won't trip, a flat circle, really soft and squishy for tummy time, and a cute border that also doesn't have any holes. I searched and searched, but I couldn't find the perfect pattern. So I decided to free-wheel it! This "pattern" is very flexible and can be changed up depending on how big you want your rug and how tight/loose you crochet, but this will give you the specifics on how I got my perfect rug and general guidelines on how you can make yours. All you need to know are single crochet, slip stitch, and how to make a magic circle, and you'll be on your way! Bernat Blanket Big Ball,Bernat Blanket Stripes

title: Halloween Humpfree
description: Hallow-in Humpfree is the second in the series of Crochet Stories by Hookanory continuing the adventures of Humpfree the Rainbow Humpty, who is feeling a little glum about having to stay in at Halloween. I wrote it hoping it will make kids (and adults perhaps) feel better about it too! This pattern has an accompanying short story that you are welcome to download and share with your gifting of this make. As an ex textiles teacher and visual learner I know many people (including myself) struggle when trying to read pattern instructions and abbreviations; So this pattern has been designed specifically with beginners in mind and includes colour coded 'long-hand' instructions so you can tick of the rounds as you go, with photo illustrations and detailed notes for making up. Hookanory is the brand name for designer Deb Ward, aiming to encourage fun, high success outcomes from simple patterns and designs. title: Spiral Hackysack
description:
These hackysacks are quick to make, plus they only use up slightly less than 10 yards, so they are perfect for random little balls of yarn you have lying around.  They make great gifts for kids and can be stuffed with polyfill for some soft balls to throw around. I realized that the technique for making the spirals might be a bit confusing.  I've also made a little tutorial to explain the first two rounds. Pattern updated: July 23, 2011 This pattern is written US terms. Additional Information Materials:
-About 10 yards each of worsted weight MC and CC.
-Size G Hook (4.0 mm)
-Split ring marker or scrap yarn
-Tapestry needle
-Pony beads (or other stuffing) Abbreviations
MC - main color
CC - contrast color
sc - single crochet
sc2tog - single crochet 2 together
rnd - round
ch - chain
pm - place marker

title: Tunisian Twist Headband
description: No prior knowledge of Tunisian stitches is required to complete this project. (This technique is sometimes called Crohooking or Croknitting too, but you do not need to know how to do this to complete this project.) This is a quick and easy pattern to create a reversible twist headband in two contrasting colors using Tunisian crochet techniques and a 2-sided crochet hook. This is a great project to use up yarn scraps and remnants. The basic headband can be made with about 60 yards of each color yarn. Beginner skill level. The pattern is easily adjustable to bigger or smaller heads. Just cast on the number of stitches to determine the width of the band and then continue the crochet rows until you reach the desired head circumference.

title: Baby Booties
description: These newborn baby booties are made in one piece (except for the drawstring at the top), so there is no seaming required. They are made from the sole to to top edge with sport weight yarn: 45 yards of the main color for the booties and 5 yards of the contrasting color for the drawstrings. These booties can easily be made with a larger hook and heavier yarn to make them in a larger size for older babies. I hope you enjoy this free baby bootie pattern. If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to ask! Check out the video on my YouTube video to learn to crochet these baby booties. My YouTube channel is "The Crochet Architect".

title: Puff and Thistle Soap Saver
description: I really love soap cozies and nothing beats a good soap bar with an amazing fragrance. I absolutely loved my Thistle Stitch Soap Saver pattern so I mixed it up with some puff stitches to make this new design as you can never have enough soap savers. It’s an easy and quick project, perfect to make small gifts for friends and family. The pattern is using different stitches to create a nice, textured look. Difficulty: Advaced beginner Size: This pattern has one size, enough to fit a standard sized soap. US terms used throughout I hope you will enjoy making it as much as I did! If you have any questions about the pattern, please feel free to contact me here or on instagram: @k.a.m.e_crochet Happy crocheting! Krisztina

Difficulty: Advaced beginner Size: This pattern has one size, enough to fit a standard sized soap. US terms used throughout I hope you will enjoy making it as much as I did! If you have any questions about the pattern, please feel free to contact me here or on instagram: @k.a.m.e_crochet Happy crocheting! Krisztina Paintbox Yarns Cotton DK

title: My Colourful Shawl
description: A simple and striking, colourful shawl with an easy fringe detail. Part of the ‘My Colourful’ range of Tunisian Crochet Patterns. The shawl is worked sideways so the stripes run the length of the shawl, created by a simple 5 row-repeating pattern. The fast colour changes are emphasised by the Tunisian Simple Stitch, resulting in an appearance that looks like the shawl has been woven. There are NO ENDS TO WEAVE IN!! The ends that you create when you begin and end your work and when you change colour are knotted together at each end to become the fringe. SIZE: 18 inches wide, 65 inches long, fringe measures 9 inches MATERIALS: 10mm Tunisian Crochet Hook, Approximately 150 yards each of 5 different chunky yarns (A, B, C, D, E) STITCHES AND TECHNIQUES: Tunisian Simple Stitch, changing colour in Tunisian Crochet for every Forward Pass and Return

title: Mr. Pistachio the dinosaur
description: When I started to crochet amigurumi of talented crochet designers' toys, I dreamed of creating new characters. I'm unable to draw. Honestly, I don't have any talent for it. My abilities of drawing is limited with chopstick men. Therefore, you shouldn't have expectations that I design a realistic animal. If I get inspired by any illustration, it wouldn't reflect me. After a while, I got an idea. If I crochet monsters, I can create whatever I want. The monster can have 3 arms, 5 eyes or a fat belly. It doesn't matter, because it is a weirdo monster. What I want is that the monsters should have a unique character, that I can write a micro story about it. This is the direction of my weirdo monsters. My first monster is Mr. Pistachio, he is a dinosaur looking like a giant pistachio. I hope you enjoy the free pattern. If you crochet Mr. Pistachio, maybe he will have a different story with you.

title: Everett's Dirt Nap
description: My husband decided to build my son, Everett, a toddler bed for his next birthday in the shape of a tractor.  He then asked me to design a blanket for this bed and "make it look like dirt, with tire tracks in it." And now you know why I named this pattern "Dirt Nap." The homespun provided the perfect color way and texture, and I finished the blanket in a matter of hours. I promise to take pictures of the bed once it is done. I only used 2 skeins of yarn in my particular blanket, but 3 would have been better for a good sized baby blanket. Do it in a green and make it look like a grassy hill, or all white for a cloud effect. This is a very easy pattern and is memorized quickly.

title: Simple Keyhole Scarf
description: SIMPLE KEYHOLE SCARF I’m currently creating pieces for a market and thought up this pattern because it’s fast, easy, unisex, and suitable for multiple ages! title: ASA Ultimate Scrapghan
description: What makes this the ultimate scrapghan? It calls for squares in sizes ranging from 7-rounds all the way down to 1-round squares. You can use up even the smallest bits. Don't like 1-round squares? Skip 'em. It's quite easy to eliminate rows of squares you don't feel like making. Want to make it longer or shorter? Add or subtract any line of same-sized squares There are both a RH- and LH-crochet join schematics. Finished size is approximately 21.3 x 16 times (11.3 x 8 times) bigger than your 3-round square. Sample measures 64” x 48” (34” x 24”)

title: Easter Eggs
description: My Easter Eggs pattern is made by crocheting pairs of matching circles using a combination of different stitches. The completed circles are sewn together like a mini cushion cover for a ready-made polystyrene egg or to fill with stuffing material to form an egg shape. I have given 3 egg sizes, Small, Medium and Big. To make an assorted collection of eggs, follow the pattern, adding the extra rounds as you go to increase the size. I used 3 x 10g balls of Scheepjes Catona in 3 different colours, although any DK cotton would work well.

title: Willow Infinity Scarf
description: The Willow Infinity Scarf Pattern is perfect for beginners and a great introduction to the herringbone double crochet stitch. It is worked in the round, so there is no sewing involved. It can be made with approximately 245 yards of bulky weight yarn or 500 yards of worsted weight yarn worked with two strands. Gauge is not super important so there is nothing to stress about and it is so easy to alter the size! My scarves measure approximately 33" long (66" circumference) and 9" wide. Adjust the length by adding or omitting stitches in the first row. Adjust the width by adding or omitting herringbone double crochet rows.

title: Ashbury Shawl
description: The Ashbury Shawl is a fun granny-stripe shawl which is very beginner friendly. Using a soft cotton DK yarn gives beautiful stitch definition but an acrylic yarn would also be great for this pattern. It is finished off with a tassel at each corner. The blanket is made with King Cole CottonSoft DK: 2 balls in Ecru 711 1 ball in Midnight 3365 1 ball in Apricot 1850 1 ball in Candy Floss 3462 However, you could have fun playing around with different colour combinations. The shawl measures 134cm along the longest edge (not including tassels) and is made with a 4 mm hook. The pattern was first published in issue 79 of Crochet Now, which was on sale from 24 February 2022.

title: Medium Wave Crochet Washcloth
description: Why would you crochet your own washcloth when you can buy a flannel? Well, they’re easy and quick to make, making them ideal projects for craft groups, TV crafting or travelling with. You can try out new techniques – like this wavy crochet stitch pattern, striping and changing colours. They’re also economical and eco friendly, made from leftover yarn and lasting for ages. This colourful, stripey version is fun to make and to use. It’s hard wearing and can be chucked in the washing if it needs freshening up. It uses any DK cotton or bamboo in three colours. It doesn’t need much of each colour so it’s a good way to use up leftover yarn.

title: Unley Chill Beanie
description: A fun beanie design that is one of my go tos for gifting and charitable donations. As it works up quickly and is easy to make, it's also a perfect market stall item. The design is unisex in appeal and the basket weave is a cosy and comfortable fabric. If you've not tried basket weave before, this is the perfect simple pattern to learn it. Top with a pom pom for a feminine touch, or follow the messy bun/ ponytail instructions for a beanie that is very much on trend. Pair the beanie with a simple basket weave scarf, or basket weave cowl. The example beanies in the images have been made with 100% wool. The wool I have used is a thickish Australian 8 ply. This is compatible with some thinner Worsted yarns. The beanie also makes up fabulously in wool mix fibres and acrylic fibres. Remember to spam me #UnleyChillBeanie @TheCrochetFix on Social Media so I can find your lovely finished objects. I hope you enjoy making your Unley Chill Beanie.

title: Foxash Square
description: Foxash is a place that only properly exists in the past. It was one of the Land Settlement Association’s horticultural sites from the 1930’s until the 1980’s. Initially to help with post WW1 unemployment, the sites became important food producers during WW2. Some greenhouses and horticultural activity still exist in the area in North Essex. Colours used Stylecraft Special DK in Silver, Grey, Charcoal, Wisteria and Violet.

title: Ringo Cat the Ami - Amigurumi Crochet Pattern
description: Difficulty Level: advanced beginner Skills required are sl st, sc, increase/decrease sts and using thread jointing method for attaching the limbs (explained in the pattern). Ringo requires minimum sewing. Head and body are crocheted as one piece, whereas the limbs are joined following the thread jointing method. Finished Size: if done with cotton fingering-weight yarn (4 ply), 2.5mm hook, it is 14 cm (5.5”) high w/o ears and 4cm (1.6”) wide w/o arms. This may vary depending on the yarn and hook size you decide to use. title: Summer Ripples
description: Summer days beckon and what better way to get ready than to crochet this adorable top for the 2-year-old in your life? The ripple stitch from Attic24 is used for the main body section and shows off a variegated yarn to great effect. The racer-style back works well for toddlers to avoid those dangling shoulder straps. Using white for the edgings gives a whole retro-feel to the variegations. The pattern is worked from the neckline down and has no seams or sewing. It was designed using a 100% cotton sports yarn – Catania by Schachenmayr. Other sports-weight yarns can be substituted but 100% cotton is advised. The pattern may not be shared or sold, but products made with this pattern may be sold.

title: Kelley's Ponchito
description: Kelley's Ponchito is a light and lacy  short poncho crocheted from side to side as a single rectangle and then seamed to create the poncho shape.  The neck opening is crocheted last.  Kelley's Ponchito offers a change from the usual wrap and shawl shapes.  However, the design can be crocheted and worn as a rectangular wrap, as well. Dimensions:  The completed and blocked piece is 31 in. (78 cm) around the top of the neck and 25 in. (63 cm) from the top to the poncho point. The following stitches were used:  chain; double crochet; extended single crochet; foundation single crochet; single crochet; slip stitch. The pattern pdf includes 6 photos and a schematic to aid you in creating the poncho shape. I am currently working on a large version of this design.

A smooth super bulky yarn and a bulky yarn with a silky eyelash texture are held together to crochet the filet mesh scarf base in double crochet and chain stiches, creating lusciously soft and wearable lace. The fringe is made from smooth, swishy mercerized cotton to create an interesting combination of textures. title: Kaleidoscope Flower Blanket
description: This pretty pattern works up quickly to make a lovely baby blanket, toddler throw rug, or older child/adult sized rug. The size can easily be adjusted to meet the individual's requirement, as from Round 10 a three round repeat applies. The pattern is suited to yarn of any fibre, however I recommend using a yarn that gives a good stitch definition. Although the example blanket (pictured) has been made with 8ply, the pattern can be worked with yarns of other ply. If you need pattern support or would like to provide feedback, please contact me via Facebook message. I hope you enjoy making your item as much I did making mine.

title: Crema, the Easter Bunny
description: This is Crema! It is a cute rabbit plush, perfect for Easter, as a keychain holder or a toy (with a small rattle inside)! It's a beginner- level friendly pattern and quick to make! YARN Cotton yarn, weight “4 ply / sport” in light blue, vanilla, magenta and pink ABBREVIATIONS (us terms) MR = magic ring ch = chain st = stitch(es) sc = single crochet inc = increase inv dec = invisible decrease (….) repeat x times = repeat instructions in the brackets x times […] = total of stitches at the end of the round SIZE 9cm tall and 6,4cm wide when using the yarn specified above NOTES & TIPS Work in continuous spiral, unless specified otherwise in the pattern It is suggested to use the "invisible decrease" in this pattern. You can alternatively use the "normal" decrease You can use any size of yarn you want; please adjust the size of the crochet hook accordingly (remember to choose half / one size smaller than the one suggested on the yarn's label )

title: Mini Cherry Bakewell
description: The Cherry Bakewell is a cousin of the Bakewell Tart, a national treasure of British baking. And of course we have Mr Kipling to thank for the iconic minis lining our supermarket shelves. I'm sure we can all agree that Mr Kipling does indeed make "exceedingly good cakes", and with this fun size crochet pattern you can hook up some exceedingly good crochet bakes too! Finished size: 6.5cm diameter x 3cm high Materials: Paintbox Yarns Simply DK in Paper White, Pillar Red, Light Caramel; crochet hook size 3.5mm; stuffing; tapestry needle for sewing in ends; scissors. The pattern is written in standard UK crochet terminology and is suitable for beginner crocheters! The stitch used throughout the pattern is the double crochet, and you can find a tutorial for this stitch right here - https://www.lovecrafts.com/en-gb/how-to-double-crochet

title: Easter Bunny And Egg Decor
description: Very quick to make if you know the basics! This pattern is available for instant download as a PDF file. It includes instructions on how to make a Easter Decor. You’ll find the written pattern along with a 2 graph and some helpful photos. This crochet Easter Decor is designed to brighten up any wall, door, window or table! You can make hang them on a wooden stick. Use different tassels, fringe and pom poms to decorate the edges of your crocheted picture. You can also make a pillowcase by simply stitching two pieces together. I made this decor of cotton yarn. You can knit from any yarn to your taste. If you enjoy Tunisian crocheting, this beautiful pattern for Easter Day is quite the showpiece! You’ll find the written pattern along with a 2 graph (Bunny and Egg), instructions how to make Tunisian simple stitch, how to change colors in Tunisian stitch crochet and how to crochet flowers for the wreath and some helpful photos.

title: Rivenhall Square
description: This square is compatible with Block a Month selected squares in that it should measure 12 inches if worked in Worsted Weight yarn, and has a stitch count of 39 sts per side. I have used Yarnsmiths Create DK in A) Pink Berry, B) Sand, C) Petal Pink and D) Canary Yellow. Rivenhall is a small village in central Essex, best know for being a service stop on the busy main road through the county. Less well known are the regular get togethers of the Essex Lacemakers which happen in the local community hall. This pdf download is safe to use and free from viruses. It has no embedded links. If you need to contact me regarding this pattern, you can do so via my user profile here on Ravelry.

title: Simplicity
description: This simple crocheted beanie features seamless construction and a contrasting brim. Only a few basic stitches are required, making it an ideal pattern for relatively new crafters. By changing colors and adding different embellishments (not included in pattern), this hat can be readily customized for a variety of festive occasions. Additional body and/or brim rounds can also be added for a slouchy look. Have fun and be sure to share your projects! Rough estimates of yarn requirements provided by my testers: Newborn - 26 yards main color and 7 yards contrast color 3-6 month - 40 yards main color and 10 yards contrast color Toddler - 70 yards main color and 12 yards contrast color Child/teen - 90 yards main color and 14 yards contrast color Adult - 105 yards main color and 16 yards contrast color

title: Shells and Chains Scarf
description: This is a very simple, beginner friendly pattern that is only a 2 row repeat. I wrote this pattern to match my "Shells & Chains Slouchy Beret"  & "Shells & Chains Half Circle Shawl" patterns. Looks equally good in a self-striping or a solid colorway. You can easily use any yarn & any gauge for the scarf, your finished sizes will vary with yarn & gauge. I have written directions for 3 widths to get you started. A small, short-ish scarf uses less than half a skein of sock yarn (40 grams were used for my sample). Many thanks to my testers, honeystalks, AussiesPrincess, CollieGrl64, bjclark63, & jam52. As always I couldn’t do it without each & every one of you. All mushrooms are easy to make with the pattern and photos provided. To make these mushrooms you need to know how to make a: chain stitch, slip stitch, single crochet, half double crochet, double crochet. title: Helina Snowflake
description: I create a new pattern every year and to be honest, it became a little tradition now! ❄️ The Helina Snowflake is a nice little gift for Christmas and a great stocking filler too. It can be used as a decoration, just make a bunch of them or create a garland and hang it on the wall, the possibilities are endless! It works up super fast and it’s easy enough for an advanced beginner as it has a full step-by-step photo tutorial, exclusively available on my blog. Difficulty: Advanced Beginner/Intermediate Finished Size: 12 cm/ 5” on widest point Materials needed: • 4 mm (G) size hook • 13 metres/15 yards (5 grams) of DK weight yarn (I used Scheepjes Twinkle in different shades) • NOTE: If you crochet loosely I advise you to use a 3.5 mm (E) size hook • Yarn needle for weaving in ends • Scissors • Stitch markers (optional) US terms used throughout. NO CHART/DIAGRAM AVAILABLE! I hope you’ll love it as much as I loved creating it! Happy crocheting! Krisztina

title: African Spirit Flamingos
description: Thank you for your interest in my African Spirit Flamingos. I designed this flamingo pattern while creating African Spirit but decided not to use it, but with you all loving the extra sections I decided to get it on the computer for you all. African Spirit Flamingos has the same stitch count as my African Spirit pattern and all the same US overlay mosaic crochet terms. 1 repeat, chain 60. 2 repeats, chain 116. 3 repeats, chain 172. 4 repeats, chain 228. title: Night Poppy
description: The pattern has 6 videos, 41 pages with 79 images that guide in detail the weaving step by step, especially for crochet makers who start in the art of 3D weaving. To facilitate reading, it has an index with links that refer directly to the chosen section without having to go through pages and pages to resume work. Although it is in English, the videos have translation of the subtitles in more than 100 languages and the written pattern has translation of the abbreviations into Spanish, Turkish, Italian, German, Portuguese, Norwegian and French. You can do it whatever your language. Night Poppy is a square, made with 3D technique, that can serve as the center of a cushion, a blanket or work several in different colors and join them. The model is made with Stylecraft special DK yarn. It can be made with Sheepjes Colour Crafter as both yarns has the same elasticity and caliber. Other yarns with similar characteristics are Studio Essential by Deramores and Astra by Patons.

title: Jellyfish
description: This crochet pattern for the Jellyfish by Sarah Quillen creates a soft huggable stuffed jellyfish that measures approx. 9" across and 11" long. The terms are US. The pattern includes an “easy to follow” layout with many pictures for reference. The pattern is designed for blanket yarn but can also be done in worsted weight yarn with a size F hook and 10mm eyes to create a much smaller jellyfish. I have chosen two yarn colors that Love Crafts has that I think will look great with this pattern but be creative and choose your favorite colors and have fun. For this pattern you will need: o Bernat Blanket Yarn o Hook size US K 10.5 / 6.5mm o Yarn needle o Stitch marker o Polyester fiberfill stuffing o 2 safety eyes (15mm)

title: Doll Shoes
description: Just like any other real girl, apart from her nice clothes, Mila the doll also needs to have matching shoes. I found many beautiful ideas for crocheted footwear for dolls on the Internet, but the specific shape of Mila’s feet urged me to come up with a unique design of shoes for her. That’s where this short tutorial emerged from. This tutorial is for shoes/booties for a sewn doll. The pattern is adaptable for larger/smaller doll feet by using thinner/thicker yarn and smaller/bigger hook. To crochet these shoes, you only need the basic level of crochet knowledge. The stitches used are single crochet. The shoes are worked in rounds, spirally, i.e. without slip stitches at the end of each round. The finishing touch is embroidering around the soles with a blanket stitch. Tied to the front are bows imitating shoe laces.

title: Vervain Throw Blanket
description: Pattern is designed to use one skein of yarn per stripe. Super bulky yarn of the same brand and line can vary slightly in thickness and yardage per color or per skein. If too much yarn is used for one stripe, use a smaller hook size for the next stripe of that color so that the yarn doesn't run out. Materials Needed Hook: 11.5mm crochet hook (U.S.P/16) Yarn: Super Bulky 6 weight yarn in necessary colors. Example uses the following colors in the line “Wool-Ease Thick & Quick Yarn” by Lion Brand in 170g skeins. a. Wheat (3 skeins) b. Petrol Blue (2 skeins) c. Eggplant (2 skeins) d. Fig (2 skeins) Notions: Scissors, tapestry needle Gage 7 st wide x 8 rows tall in single crochet for a 4”(10cm) by 4”(10cm) swatch

title: Baby Polar Bear Beau
description: Baby Polar Bear Beau is part of Project Arctic. In Project Arctic I crochet different animals that live on or around the poles. They may live on the ice, in the air or in the water. What makes those animals special, is that many of them have a very different appearance when they’re young compared to when they are grown up. And that is very nice to crochet! The polar bear is the largest bear species living in the Arctic circle on the ice and in the sea. Did you know that polar bears spend hours waiting at a seal’s breathing hole for a seal to come up? If they are fast enough they drag the seal up on the ice with a forepaw and kill it.

title: Southwest Skies Throw
description: Everyone will want to snuggle under this beginner-friendly blanket in soft, fluffy super bulky yarn. This simple pattern is all double crochet (US terms) in between the stitches. So easy! This is a great pattern for crocheting in dark rooms, while watching television or children, on road trips, or for crocheters with limited sight. Photo tutorial included in the pattern. Pattern is written in US terms and includes explanations of all stitches and abbreviations. US and metric measurements given. Requires only chain and double crochet stitches. Finished dimensions are about 40 by 66 inches (102 by 168 cm) when using super bulky weight yarn and an 8 mm hook. Requires about 880 yards (804 meters) of super bulky yarn. The sample blanket used 4 cakes of Bernat Blanket Ombré yarn (100% polyester; 220 yards (201 m) per 300-gram cake). One cake of Navy Ombre, one cake of Shaded Blue Ombre, one cake of Ocean Teal Ombre, and one cake of Violet Ombre.

title: Yellow Duckling
description:
This yellow duckling is a duck on a mission! Once completed, he can’t wait but to leave the house and spread the word about something really important! Intrigued? Visit www.thelittleyellowduckproject.org for more details and start making your own duck right away! Or just use the pattern to make some cute gifts for family and friends. He’s quick to make and sure to bring smile to anyone who spots him! The duck is worked top down with head and body being made together and beak, wings and feet sewn on later. It’s also worked in continuous rounds so there’s no need to join the rounds, but a stitch marker might be helpful. The pattern contains a handy printable version and goes in two versions - US and UK terminology. Feel free to join Cute and Kaboodle’s Ravelry Group !
We have CALs and games and giveaways - hope you’ll come and play!
You can also show your finished ducks there!

title: Tunisian Mermaid Tail
description: I fell in love with Tunisian Crochet a few years ago, and have enjoyed creating various items with the style. Yarn Used: Elle Chick Double Knit, Colour Vibrant, 100g, approx. 250m/275yds, 7 Skeins. Guage: 18 Stitches and 10 Rows (10 x Forward Pass and Return Pass) = 10cm/4” in Slanted Stitch Pattern Hooks Used:  Standard Tunisian Afghan Hook Size 7mm, Extended Tunisian Hook Size 7mm, Standard Crochet Hook 7mm Finished Size:   Body:  94cm long excluding the fin Width at the top:  90cm Fin:  40 cm long This blanket has a double layered fin which is sturdy and holds its shape much better than a single layer, it also creates an extra space inside the fin where the feet can go in creating quite a life like mermaid tail if the person using it wiggles their feet inside.  This blanket is large enough for an adult or teenager, but easily adjustable in size so for a small child by working less rows on the Main Body slanted stitch section.

title: Vertical Stripe Quick Scarf
description: I love linen stitch! It’s so easy and quick, and with this hefty yarn you can make a whole scarf in very little time at all. I got two scarves out of one ball, but you could make a wider or longer one if you liked. I chose to do it long-ways, to get the vertical stripe, partly because that’s a bit unusual and looks good and partly because the colour change on this yarn (which I bought on a whim) is quite long, so if I did it the other way it would be big blocks of colour with sudden changes, which I didn’t want.

title: Granny Stipe Cowl
description: The Granny Stripe Cowl pattern is a super easy and fast to work up project, it is ideal for using up small leftovers from other projects. Although you need 5 colours to make the same design as the sample, you would easily use more or fewer colours and customise it for stash busting! A beginner level pattern with a couple of easy challenges like back loop only and crocheting in the round. This pattern is a written pattern. It is written in US crochet terms and to complete it you will need to be able to do the following stitches, double crochet, chain, skip stitches, slip stitches, single crochet, single crochet back loop only. You will need a 4mm crochet hook and 5 colours of DK acrylic yarn (I used Robin DK) for colours 2,3 and 4 you will need approximately 30g of each colour and for colours 1 and 5 you will need approximately 45g. Gauge in pattern of cluster stitch is 15 x 10.5 Dimensions of the cowl are 28 inches around the neck and 17 inches high.

Difficulty Level: beginner  Skills required are sl st, sc, increase/decrease sts, change colour, sewing multiple pieces together. Little Cat is crocheted in all his parts as a safety feature for young children. Feel free to do otherwise if this is for an older child if you wish. Finished Size: if done with cotton fingering-weight yarn (4 ply) in the selected colours for all items, 3mm hook, it is 10cm (3.9”) tall w/o ears. This may vary depending on the yarn and hook size you decide to use. If you happen to have any questions during the execution of this pattern please feel free to contact me through this website or through the contact details included in the pattern. Thanks for looking at my pattern!  Mary

title: Aspen Blanket
description: The Aspen blanket is thick and chunky--perfect for tummy-time or adding comfort to hard surfaces. For beginner blankets, I love writing patterns where we can easily check to make sure we are placing right stitch in the right place. The sc will be placed in the dc, and the dc will be placed in the sc. If you wish to increase or decrease the width, the pattern is a multiple of 2 +1. If you enjoyed this pattern, please check out my other designs or follow me on Instagram to see what I’m up to :)

title: Petunia Part 1
description: Petunia is a cushion made with 3D technique. Uniting several, you can make a soft and warm blanket. The pattern has 2 parts. The first has 3 videos, 29 pages with 33 images that guide in detail the weaving step by step, especially for crochet makers who start in the art of 3D weaving. To facilitate the work the pattern contains an index with links that refer directly to the chosen section without having to go through pages and pages to resume work. Although it is in English, the videos have translation of the subtitles in more than 50 languages and the written pattern has translation of the abbreviations into Spanish, Turkish, Italian, German, Portuguese, Norwegian and French. You can do it whatever your language. Happy knitting!

title: Pastel Pops Pillow
description: My Pastel Pops pillow is a larger version of my pink ombre pops pillow. It was one of the first patterns I released, and it is still one of my favorites because the popcorn stitches give it a nice texture, and it works up quickly with worsted weight yarn. The pillow was 14 by 14 inches, and I have been waiting to make a larger version of it. Hence, this pastel pops pillow is an 18 by 18 inch granny square pillow with a pastel popcorn center. However, you can use any colors you would like! The back is a traditional granny square, and the bottom of the pillow is held together with five buttons, so the pillowcase can be taken on and off of the insert. I used Blue Sky Fibers organic cotton yarn; however, any worsted weight yarn will work! The pattern includes some pictures to explain the steps. I hope you enjoy the pattern!

title: Baby Ripple Onesie
description: An adorable onesie for a 3 or 6-month-old baby in double knitting. I love the neat ripple stitch that Lucy from Attic24 uses on of her patterns and I really wanted to do a stripy baby romper, so I’ve combined the 2 in this cute baggy pattern (with plenty of nappy room). The opening extends down both legs to help with changing. I hate sewing up at the end of a project, so I always try to avoid seams. This design has no sewing apart from adding the buttons. The ripple effect shows up best with a variegated yarn or in stripes. I love the colour patterning that comes out of the Baby Blossom DK by Hayfield and highly recommend it, especially with a white yarn for the yoke, cuffs & edging. I have also worked it using Innocence Bamboo Rich DK by James C Brett in stripes of pastel rainbow colours – more ends to sew in but who doesn’t love a rainbow? Working the pattern in one colour would be a more subtle effect.

title: Sparkle Motion Crochet Headband
description: This is a simple pattern for beginners ! It’s so easy, and that’s why it’s free! I love the movie Donnie Darko, and for some reason, when I was making this, it reminded me of the little girl dance troupe . So, it’s amply named, Sparkle Motion. This is a headband/ear warmer and uses only single crochet (sc) and double crochet (dc) stitches. Materials: Hook: G Yarn:  Premier Yarns Isaac Mizrahi - Broadway yarn in Imperial colorway (RED) - Glitter, Sparkle Worsted weight (4) Hope you enjoy this free pattern! It’s easy, and great for beginners. I used a knitting machine to make a matching scarf and I love it! (I’m not a knitter - LOL - hence, the machine) :).

title: Slightly Slouchy Hat
description: A crochet pattern for a basic hat with just a little slouch, that can be easily adjusted to suite the wearers style. Crocheted from the top down, you can adjust the length as you please. A simple crochet cord threaded through stitches in the back gathers the fabric and lets you adjust the amount of slouch. Pattern is offered in US/Canada version and UK/Australia/New Zealand version; both PDFs are included. Please check the top right corner of the pattern pages if you want to be sure you are using the version with the terminology you prefer. Note that if using Twisted Fiber Arts Playful Evolution , a size medium uses up almost every inch of yarn and includes the entire set of color changes. For smaller sizes, to include all the color changes you may wish to wind off a few yards of the beginning of the skein to start further into the first color. For longer hats and sizes large and extra large, Twisted Fiber Arts sells coordinating color 1/4 skeins and full skeins.

For longer hats and sizes large and extra large, Twisted Fiber Arts sells coordinating color 1/4 skeins and full skeins.

title: Hipster Preemie Hat
description: Recently I discovered "Bosnian crochet", also known as "slip stitch crochet". A fold-up brim also can accommodate various heights as needed. Please check with your chosen NICU about their needs before donating. Thank you. This pattern uses US crochet terms.

title: Coin Purse with Button - US Version
description: A simple little crocheted purse made in one piece– starting from the bottom up, in a spiral. The flap is then worked flat without breaking off the yarn and includes a button loop. Pattern Format : Pattern is written in US crochet terminology (GB available as separate download) and includes photos and mini tutorials for some of the techniques involved. Step by step video tutorial also available - link in pattern. Gauge: Specific gauge is not required, however you are looking to achieve a relatively dense and firm fabric to attain the correct structure for the purse. Finished dimensions: Aran / Worsted – approx. 4”/10cm tall & 4.5”/11cm wide Chunky – approx. 5”/12.75cm tall & 6”/15cm wide Difficulty: Easy - magic ring (instructions given), single crochet, working in the round, increasing and decreasing (instructions given), working flat in double crochet, basic sewing skills (attaching a button), keeping track of rounds with stitch marker.

title: Japanese Knot Produce Bag
description: I grew up carrying my own grocery bags, but for some reason it never clicked that I could replace those flimsy plastic bags from the produce section. Well, it finally did, and I've been making these little things for a while now. They work up in no time. Depending on the size they weigh between 5 and 19 grams; and I always have 3 or 4 of them balled up in my grocery bag. ("Machine washable, Daaahling. It's a new feature.") No more plastic bags! Please copy and pass this pattern around freely, spread the word, make them for your non-crocheting friends (whaaat?), and let's get rid of as many plastic bags as we can.

title: Verbena Mesh Cropped Vest
description: Stitch count matches the example top (size M) but measurements will also be included to easily adjust to the desired sizing. It is recommended to use body measurements or measurements from the non-stretch garment that has the desired fit. Construction consists of a front and back piece that are sewn together. The front and back pieces are made from the bottom up. There are a few variations on the mesh stitch, but in the pattern there are instructions to use chain and treble crochet stitches. When the “mesh stitch” or “mesh sts” is used in the pattern, the term is describing the number squares these stitches make. So “ch 3, skip 3 chs and tc into fourth ch” makes one square and is one mesh stitch in this pattern.

title: Vintage Vibes Baby Beanie
description: A super cute baby beanie. You’ll find that these beanies fly off the hook. Make them as gifts, for your market stall, or for charity. Pair them with a pretty filet baby blanket. Top them with a pom pom and add earflaps for some extra cuteness. Use one or more colours to add your personal touch to the design. Instructions for a number of design variations are provided. description: Welcome to my free Butterfly Spirit design that’s 109 stitches by 67/69 rows. There are 3 different patterns of butterflies When repeating the patterns start the repeat with row 2. title: Good Old Fashioned Bunting

There is nothing quite like homemade bunting to add some vintage flare to your celebrations. Whether it be a baby shower, Birthday party, wedding, Summer fete or simply a decorative piece to hang in your bedroom, there's no denying that bunting has charm!
We've come up with a pattern for some classic crocheted bunting, which we've quite aptly named; Good Old Fashioned Bunting. We hope enjoy our version of this timeless classic! Materials Yarn
Scissors
Crochet hook
Darning needle
Pins (for optional, but recommend blocking)
Starch (for optional, but recommend blocking) Yarn and Hook Cotton works great for this project, but any type of yarn can be used. We recommend going down half a hook size to the recommendation of the yarn manufacturer (This is so that your bunting works up a little tighter and holds its shape better). title: Mini heart crochet pattern, easy crochet Valentine's Day gift
description: This mini heart crochet pattern is a great project for crochet beginners. You can use it as a keychain and of course it will be an excellent Valentine's Day gift. This is a quick crochet project. You can make it in whatever colour you like and the pattern is great for plush/chenille yarn! Whether you are new to crochet or have been crocheting for years, this pattern is designed to be accessible to those of all skill levels with detailed instructions, pictures and links to VIDEO tutorials. Size of the finished toy: About 1.58 x 1.58 inches (4 x 4 cm). You can use the same pattern to make larger or smaller toys by using finer or bulkier yarn. Pick a crochet hook at least a size smaller than suggested on yarn label and crochet tightly enough to achieve a tight gauge that will not allow the stuffing to show through the fabric. ⚡️Difficulty: Easy For this pattern you need to have basic crochet and amigurumi skills and to know how to read crochet patterns.

title: Colour Splash Cardi
description: This pattern is suitable for a beginner looking for a bit of a challenge. The pattern requires basic knowledge of crochet stitches: slip knot, slip stitch, chain, single crochet, double crochet and is written in US crochet terms. You will need: 5 mm crochet hook, yarn needle, stitch markers, scissors, aran/worsted weight yarn. I used Deramores STUDIO Anti-Pilling Aran: approximately 500 g azalea (COLOUR A), 300 g caramel (COLOUR B), 250 g punch (COLOUR C), 50 g watermelon (COLOUR D) and 50 g juniper (COLOUR E). My tension is 3 V stitch/ 2 row square = 6 x 6 cm. Finished cardigan is ONE size fits most, photos shown are modelled on UK size 14/16 and 8.

title: Gardening Hat
description: This is “Ken’s hat”, which was a simpler pattern for my husband, adapted to have a brim for me. It is cool and comfortable for the summer, shields your eyes from the sun and could be made brighter or decorated, but as it is it will do for gardening! The brim is made more stable by turning up at the edge, so it doesn’t flop – if you prefer floppy, just don’t put the turnup on it. It does cope with a bit of a rain shower, but I don’t know how well it would do in a real downpour, as I haven’t worn it in one yet. As soon as I wore it, though, I was asked for one, and I had bought two ‘balls’ of the yarn in case I needed a second, which I didn’t, so I was able to make another right away and I was able to take a picture of the two.

title: The Elsie Mitts

Each mitt is identical. “The Elsie Mitts” are part of a series of free patterns that I am offering on Ravelry. These fingerless mitts are both knitted and crocheted. The body of the mitts are knitted and then the thumb gusset is crocheted. These mitts are very easy and are quick-to-make fingerless mitts, with the two mitts being identical. title: Mesh Soap Bag
description: This simple, cotton, mesh bag features a crocheted lucet tie-cord and a hanging loop to help your soap dry out between uses and keep it out of the direct flow of water when used in the shower. It’s also a great way to store and use up small, leftover pieces of soap. The pattern includes two sizes. The large soap bag is designed to fit a 6cm x 11cm x 2.5cm bar of soap & the small soap bag fits a 8cm x 5cm x 3cm however, the beauty of mesh means it’s very stretchy – either size would easily fit a smaller or even slightly larger bar of soap. Once you’ve mastered the basic principle, you can easily customise the size of the bag. In the same way, although the pattern is written for DK yarn you could easily switch to Aran or Worsted weight with an appropriate hook for a slightly larger size. The pattern features written instructions as well as charts, photos and links to YouTube video tutorials for the picot stitches and lucet cord.

title: Apple plush
description: This is an easy to follow pattern to crochet an apple plush. The apple ends up being about 3 inches (7.5cm) tall not including the stem and roughly is the size of a large apple in your hand. It is crochet from the bottom up and then shaped into its apple shape using the thread from the stem. Not much yarn is needed. You can also make this with any weight yarn, that just changes the final size of it. It doesn't matter as long as the stitches are consistent throughout the construction of the piece. I've just given what I've used in the yarn weight and hook size as an example.

title: Isabella - Crocodile Stitch Baby Mittens
description: ## ISABELLA - CROCODILE STITCH BABY MITTENS ## SIZE 0-3 MONTHS A very cute little pair of ribbed mittens with an adjustable crocodile stitch cuff. Should easily fit 0-3month old babies (fit my 3month old son no problem at all) and because of the adjustable cuff can be used as scratch mitts or as winter warmers over a coat cuff. The pair shown have been worked using 1 skein of Sirdar Calico DK in shade Miami 727. The Calico is beautifully soft without being fluffy so should not irritate your little one. They are also very cute if made using the Sirdar Smiley Stripes or any soft double knit weight yarn.

Please wear it in remembrance of this incredible woman who lived an incredible life. The collar is easy to replicate using basic stitches. It can easily be created in different sizes as desired. The pattern is suitable for a beginner crocheter with basic crochet skills. The artist/designer, Kristen Stein, retains all copyrights on this particular design. Please do not redistribute or reprint without the expressed written consent of the author/designer. If you create an item using this pattern and share it on your site or on social media, please provide credit by referencing my name and pattern. Thank you. The collar has : Inner diameter of about 5.5"(13cm); outer diameter of about 11"(27.5cm) and the crocheted band is about 3"(7.5cm) in width.

title: Lovely Linen Frame
description: Adding a personal touch to your home décor or gift wrap with a crochet frame is a great way to let your personal style shine through. I love to use crochet frames for photos, upcycled greeting cards, or even as gift tags. There are many free crochet patterns for crochet frames online and I'm adding this one to the mix. NOTE: I have not included yardage or gauge for this project as it is easily adjusted to accommodate the yarn, hook, and frame base that you are using. For this particular frame I wanted something on the larger side, so I used 4 ply linen thread held double and an inexpensive, (£2), 6 inch wooden embroidery hoop from a discount store for my frame. The hoop is probably terrible for actual embroidery but great for this purpose, and you can make two frames using both pieces of the hoop. You can use any weight yarn and a variety of materials for the base of your frame – just adjust hook size and beginning number of stitches accordingly. Inexpensive bangle brac

title: Doorknob Butler
description: Pattern is FREE until Monday, December 5, 2011. With a big crochet hook, thick woolly yarn and about 20 minutes of your time, create a silent, charming assistant to protect your inner sanctum or someone else's. It's a nice last minute gift for someone who needs it. The scrapbooking sections of craft stores have fun alphabet brads that you can use to create an instant message. Make more than one bearing different messages: one skein of the recommended yarn yields 2 or 3 Doorknob Butlers. The best messages for this project are short and sweet (texting and email abbreviations can help!). Besides “Nap Time” or "Sleepy Baby" seen here, some other possibilities are “Me Time” or “Spa Time” for harried moms; “Pls Knock” or “Stay Out” especially for teens; “Baby Zzz” or “No Waky” for a nursery. For a home office, maybe “At Work,” “Office Time,” “On the Clock,” “On Phone,” “Busy Bee,” even “Love You” when family feels neglected

title: Bunny and Bear Buddies
description:
You think you can easily tell them apart, but in fact these two are identical twins  made from exactly the same pattern! The bunnys ears just grew a little
The only parts that are different for these two are ears and tails. This file has three pattern sections: bunny and bear  for parts that are the same for both, bunnys (for bunny ears and tail) and bear (for bears ears and tail). Then, the assembling part is again for both animals. The samples were worked in Knit Picks Brava Worsted. A substitute yarn is shown below.

title: Wicket the Ewok
description:
This pattern comes with two ways of making the head - one with an embroidered face, one using mohair yarn to give a fluffy face. Hood is made separately. The pattern is written using US crochet terms, and the list of abbreviations includes the UK alternatives.
You will need the following yarn,  double knitting weight:
Brown yarn, something a bit tweedy will look good.
Cream yarn.
Russet brown yarn.
Light grey/brown yarn (for head version 1) or light grey/brown mohair yarn (for head version 2).
Small amount of brown and white yarn or embroidery thread for nose and mouth. Height approx 5.5" tall.

title: Easy Crochet Soap Sack
description: Soap sacks are small bags that you can pop a bar of soap in. The loop on top allows you hang the soap to dry. The fabric of the bag itself acts as a washcloth or mitt and a way to keep the bar in between uses. This is from a Charity Project started by Stacy who worked with Lion Brand yarns to create crochet and knit soap sack patterns found on their website. I found that the Lion Brand Crocheted Soap SACK I made did not dry out but was damp long after coming out of the dryer (and additional time hanging on the clothesline), so I looked through other patterns and decided to design my own Soap SACK to donate to the food pantry in my city. These simple crocheted sacks take me about an hour to make one.  I used leftover balls of Sugar 'n Cream for my bags. The soap sack idea is to place a wrapped bar of soap inside and donate it to a local food bank, shelter, or mission. The person who receives it now has a wash mitt and a bar of soap, as well as a holder to keep the soap in between uses.

title: Retro Shell Shawl
description: Hello, I call this my retro shell shawl because I’ve been making it since the early 70s, when I was about 13 years old. I expect I had a pattern for it once, maybe, but it has been changed and adapted and forgotten and re-worked-out and remade so many times since then I’m sure it’s mine now. I made one for a friend, then was asked for another by another friend, then made myself one, then said yes, I’d make more if people bought me the yarn, then started charging to make them! Over the years I must have made dozens, they go out of fashion and come back in and I find one, wear it and it starts again. Recently I dug one out, worked out a pattern again, made some and here it is for you to try. Enjoy.

title: Preemie Bonding Square
description: The crochet basketweave bonding square is a pattern that I specially designed for the Preemie Crochet Challenge hosted by Helen and Sara from Sunflower Cottage Crochet. PLEASE NOTE THAT BY TAKING THE FREE PDF PATTERN YOU ARE PLEDGING TO DONATE AT LEAST ONE ITEM MADE FROM THAT PATTERN TO A SUITABLE CHARITY / UNIT / FAMILY. **What is a Preemie Bonding Square?**A bonding square is a small crocheted or knitted square that the mother of a preemie baby wears for a few hours in direct contact with her skin. This way, the square will take up the smell of the mother. After this time, the square will be placed in the bed of the preemie baby so that he/she will get to know the smell of the mother. The squares will be swapped between the mother and the baby until the preemie is ready to finally return home.

title: Elloth Granny Square

title: Seaside Rendezvous
description: ITA Seaside Rendezvous è uno scialle top-down; si lavora in righe di andata e ritorno partendo da un anello di catenelle centrale. È alla portata delle principianti, dal momento che per realizzarlo è sufficiente conoscere solamente maglia alta e catenella. ENG Seaside Rendezvous is a top-down shawl: starting from a chain ring, it is worked in rows. It is beginner-friendly: to crochet this project, you have to know chain stitch and US double crochet only (UK treble). DUTCH Seaside Rendez-vous is een shawl gehaakt van boven naar beneden: beginnend met een ring lossen; er wordt gehaakt in toeren. Het is zeker een werkstuk voor beginners: om dit project te kunnen maken moet je vertrouwd zijn met de kettingsteek (losse) en de (US) stokjes (vasten voor UK).

title: Deceptive
description: It is crocheted from bottom to top, parallelogram wrap. An increase was made at one end and decrease at the other end. There are options to make it longer/wider if you desire. Sizes are 32” to 36” and 38” to 42”. Any yarn will be great as long as the wrap turns out drapey. Any colours will be great too. title: Rainbow Skies Headband
description: This headband is chunky, warm, and simple. The pattern uses chain (ch) and double crochets (dc). Michael‘s carries this yarn – or use any chunky 6 weight you like! This is measured on individual head circumference. The best way to do this is with a flexible tape measure. Measure from your forehead, down and around the nape of your neck. The headband in the pattern measures approximately 20” or 51 cm prior to joining ends. This allows for stretch – my head measures 22″ and it fits perfect. FREE and easy pattern

title: Highlander Cowl
description: This is probably the simplest pattern I’ll ever write, but it actually took a lot of experimentation to get the floppy drape I was after, using crochet rather than knitted Garter stitch. A LARGE (size Q, 15.75mm) hook made all the difference; the 11.5mm P hook was just too tight! I tried many different stitch patterns, eventually coming up with a sc-through-back-loop with a chain stitch between. Wear yours as you travel through time, or wherever your road takes you!

This eye-catching pattern with a bold geometric pattern stitched in Soho Skwishy yarn measure 15" across, making it perfect for storing plushies, toys, or other large projects. The intermediate crochet basket pattern uses elongated stitches to create the unique stair-step look with a subtle texture. You can easily adjust the height of the basket to boost the storage space, but keep in mind that will require an adjustment to the yarn and finishing materials used as an interior support and lining. Skill Level: Intermediate Size: 15" (38 cm) across base 8" (20.3cm) tall Materials: Soho Skwishy • 1019 Moonbeam—3 balls • 1009 Papaya—1 ball • 1010 Sunny Side—1 ball Hook: US size L-11 (8mm) crochet hook or size needed to obtain gauge Extras: Yarn needle, stitch marker, tapestry needle, four 12 x 18" sheets of 3mm craft foam, ¾ yard of quilting fabric, tape, foam-safe adhesive spray, sewing needle and matching thread
